This is a compliance requirement for CCPA data deletion (Risk Register Item 10).

Integration-specific cleanup: (a) Call Google Calendar channels.stop for all active watches, (b) Delete Microsoft Graph subscriptions via DELETE /subscriptions/{id} for all active Outlook email/calendar watches, (c) Remove SendGrid Inbound Parse webhook configuration, (d) Revoke JobTread grant key, (e) Cancel Stripe subscription via API. Each integration adapter implements a disconnect(tenantId) method.

Tenant provisioning saga: Tenant provisioning is implemented as a saga: (1) Create Supabase tenant row with RLS-enabled records, (2) Configure NATS subject namespace (application-layer subject prefix TENANT_{id}.>), (3) Verify tenant key prefix in Valkey (tenant:{id}:*). Note: Per-tenant NKey credentials and Valkey ACL users are added post-100-tenant scale. If any step fails, preceding steps are rolled back. A health check verifies the new tenant can publish/receive a test NATS message before onboarding is marked complete.

Integration API Reality (Researched Mar 2026)

ServiceAuthAPI TypeRate LimitFree TierKey Gotcha
JobTreadAPI KeyRESTUndocumentedTrialLimited support — won’t debug your code
QuickBooks OnlineOAuth 2.0REST100 req/10sSandboxRefresh token expires after 100 days of non-use
Google CalendarOAuth 2.0RESTGenerousYesfreebusy scope = no event titles (privacy by design, perfect for spec)
Google DriveOAuth 2.0REST10 req/sYesShared drives don’t allow external sharing
HubSpot (CRM)OAuth 2.0REST100 req/10sFree tierFree sufficient for lead capture
OpenWeatherMapAPI KeyREST60 req/min1K calls/dayReturns UTC, need local timezone conversion
Gmail (send)OAuth 2.0RESTGenerousYesgmail.send (sensitive scope) — drafts stored in Kiptra UI, not Gmail drafts folder
Outlook EmailOAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph)RESTGenerousYesMail.Send + Mail.Read (standard delegated scopes — no security assessment needed, simpler than Google’s sensitive scope review)
Outlook CalendarOAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph)RESTGenerousYesCalendars.Read (standard delegated scope). Webhook subscriptions max ~3 days (vs Google’s 7); renew every 2 days via BullMQ cron
SendGrid (inbound)API KeyWebhookUnlimited100/dayNeeds MX record for leads@ email parsing

Critical Integration Decisions:

  • QB read-only enforcement: QB API allows writes, but Slate spec forbids it. Enforce at app layer with read-only wrapper.
  • Email pattern: Gmail API (gmail.send scope) or Microsoft Graph (Mail.Send scope) for outbound email + SendGrid Inbound Parse for leads@ inbox. Drafts are stored in the Kiptra drafts table for human approval, not in the customer’s email drafts folder. Customer chooses Google or Microsoft during onboarding. Agent code is provider-agnostic via EmailProvider / CalendarProvider interfaces. No single tool does both inbound and outbound.
  • Calendar privacy: Google: Use calendar.freebusy scope (no event details visible) + calendar.events scope for creating consultation events. Microsoft: Use Calendars.Read scope via Microsoft Graph.
  • Microsoft 365 provider abstraction: All email and calendar operations go through provider-agnostic interfaces (EmailProvider, CalendarProvider) with implementations for both Google (GoogleEmailProvider, GoogleCalendarProvider) and Microsoft (MicrosoftEmailProvider, MicrosoftCalendarProvider). Agent code never calls provider APIs directly.
  • Drive structure: Shared Drive per customer → Project folders → Room → Category (Submittals, Cut Sheets, Warranties).

5b. Core Platform DDL (Complete Schema)

Complete CREATE TABLE definitions for all core platform tables referenced in Section 5. Every tenant-scoped table includes RLS policies using the (SELECT current_setting('app.tenant_id', true))::uuid pattern with FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY. Platform-scoped tables are explicitly annotated with NO RLS.

Convention alignment: These DDL statements follow the same patterns established in the Business OS schema (Section 15.5) and K&B schema (Section 16.8): UUID PKs with gen_random_uuid(), snake_case plural table names, TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), CHECK constraints for enum-like TEXT columns, and composite indexes on (tenant_id, <query_column>).

Table Summary (20 tables + 2 helper functions)

DomainTableScopePurpose
Auth & TenancyusersPlatformUser profiles extending better-auth auth.users
tenantsPlatformTop-level organizational unit (scope root)
tenant_membersTenantUser-to-tenant RBAC role mapping
invitationsTenantPending team invitations with token-based acceptance
Agents & ExecutionagentsTenantActive agent instances per tenant
agent_executionsTenantFull run history with status lifecycle (9 states)
agent_prompt_versionsPlatformPrompt version history with canary rollout support
conversation_turnsTenantAI conversation threads with tool call records
Human-in-the-LoopescalationsTenantApproval queue for agent decisions requiring review
draftsTenantAgent-generated content pending human approval
Event Bus & Webhooksevent_bus_logTenantFull NATS JetStream event audit trail
webhook_eventsTenantInbound webhook deduplication (7-day TTL)
IntegrationsintegrationsTenantOAuth connections with AES-256-GCM encrypted tokens
Supportsupport_conversationsTenantSupport Agent chat sessions
support_messagesTenantIndividual messages with confidence scores and KB citations
Billing & Usagetenant_usage_dailyTenantPre-aggregated daily usage metrics per agent type
NotificationsnotificationsTenantIn-app notifications via Socket.io
notification_preferencesTenantPer-user, per-category channel preferences
Securitytenant_keysPlatformEnvelope encryption DEKs (service-role only)
Configurationsystem_configPlatformPlatform-wide key-value configuration

Full DDL

SQL
-- =============================================================================
-- Kiptra AI — Core Platform DDL
-- Complete schema for all core platform tables (Plan v3)
-- Generated: 2026-03-21
--
-- Conventions (matching existing Business OS/K&B DDL):
--   - UUID PKs with gen_random_uuid()
--   - snake_case, plural table names
--   - TIMESTAMPTZ for all timestamps, DEFAULT now()
--   - RLS via current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid wrapped in SELECT
--   - CHECK constraints for enum-like TEXT columns
--   - Composite indexes on (tenant_id, <query_column>)
--   - FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all tenant-scoped tables
--   - Platform-scoped tables explicitly noted with NO RLS
-- =============================================================================


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: AUTH & TENANCY
-- =============================================================================

-- Users table: extends better-auth's auth.users with platform-specific fields.
-- better-auth manages the auth.users table directly; this table stores
-- additional profile data that better-auth does not handle.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE users (
  id            UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">REFERENCES auth.users ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  email         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">UNIQUE,
  name          TEXT,
  avatar_url    TEXT,
  mfa_enabled   BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false,
  email_verified BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false, -- better-auth may manage this in auth.users; kept here for API contract alignment
  lASt_logASs="kw">IN_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at    TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

-- No RLS on users — access controlled at application layer via better-auth.
-- Users span tenants (a user can belong to multiple tenants via tenant_members).
COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE users IS ASs="str">'PlatfASs="kw">ORm user profiles extASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg better-auth auth.users. No RLS — cross-tenant by design.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_users_email ASs="kw">ON users (email);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_users_lASt_logASs="kw">IN ASs="kw">ON users (lASt_logASs="kw">IN_at);


-- Tenants: the top-level organizational unit. Every customer (individual or team)
-- is a tenant. All tenant-scoped data references this table.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE tenants (
  id                      UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  name                    TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  slug                    TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">UNIQUE,
  ASs="kw">INdustry_vertical       TEXT,                -- e.g., ASs="str">'cASs="kw">ONstructiASs="kw">ON_remodelASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'hvac', ASs="str">'plumbASs="kw">INg'
  company_size            TEXT
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (company_size ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'solo', ASs="str">'small', ASs="str">'medium', ASs="str">'large')),
  plan                    TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'starter'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (plan ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'starter', ASs="str">'professiASs="kw">ONal', ASs="str">'enterprise')),
  billASs="kw">INg_status          TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'active'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (billASs="kw">INg_status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'active', ASs="str">'pASt_due', ASs="str">'cancelASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'canceled')),
  stripe_customer_id      TEXT,
  stripe_subscriptiASs="kw">ON_id  TEXT,
  feature_flags           JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'{}',
  ASs="kw">ONboardASs="kw">INg_step         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'company'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (ASs="kw">ONboardASs="kw">INg_step ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'company', ASs="str">'profile', ASs="str">'plan', ASs="str">'payment', ASs="str">'team', ASs="str">'complete')),
  ASs="kw">ONboardASs="kw">INg_completed    BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false,
  timezASs="kw">ONe                TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'America/Chicago',
  industry_vertical       TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'kitchen_bath'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (industry_vertical ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'kitchen_bath', ASs="str">'hvac', ASs="str">'general_construction', ASs="str">'landscaping')),
  tenant_type             TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'contractor'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_type ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'contractor', ASs="str">'dealer', ASs="str">'sales_rep', ASs="str">'manufacturer')),
  created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at              TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

-- No tenant-scoped RLS on tenants itself — the tenant row IS the scope root.
-- Access controlled at application layer (better-auth Organization plugin).
COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE tenants IS ASs="str">'Top-level ASs="kw">ORganizatiASs="kw">ONal unit. Every customer is a tenant. No self-referencASs="kw">INg RLS.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">UNIQUE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenants_slug ASs="kw">ON tenants (slug);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenants_plan ASs="kw">ON tenants (plan);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenants_billASs="kw">INg_status ASs="kw">ON tenants (billASs="kw">INg_status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenants_stripe_customer ASs="kw">ON tenants (stripe_customer_id) ASs="kw">WHERE stripe_customer_id IS ASs="kw">NOT NULL;
ASs="kw">CREATE INDEX idx_tenants_type ASs="kw">ON tenants (tenant_type);
ASs="kw">CREATE INDEX idx_tenants_vertical ASs="kw">ON tenants (industry_vertical);

-- B2B Network Layer (post-launch — see Section 6a Multi-Persona Architecture)
-- tenant_relationships: bilateral business connections between tenants.
-- cross_tenant_orders: orders flowing between connected tenants.
-- shared_catalogs: manufacturer catalogs distributed to authorized dealers.
-- Full DDL in kiptra-multi-persona-architecture.md. Tables created when B2B features ship.

-- Tenant members: maps users to tenants with RBAC roles.
-- Managed via better-auth Organization plugin; this table is the source of truth
-- for who belongs to which tenant and with what role.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE tenant_members (
  id          UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id   UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id     UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  role        TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'viewer'
              ASs="kw">CHECK (role ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'owner', ASs="str">'admASs="kw">IN', ASs="str">'manager', ASs="str">'designer', ASs="str">'bookkeeper', ASs="str">'viewer')),
  is_active   BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT true,
  ASs="kw">ONboardASs="kw">INg_completed BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false,
  ASs="kw">INvited_by  UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  accepted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (tenant_id, user_id)
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE tenant_members ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE tenant_members ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON tenant_members
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE tenant_members IS ASs="str">'Maps users to tenants with RBAC roles. RLS enfASs="kw">ORces tenant isolatiASs="kw">ON.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenant_members_tenant ASs="kw">ON tenant_members (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenant_members_user ASs="kw">ON tenant_members (user_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenant_members_role ASs="kw">ON tenant_members (tenant_id, role);


-- Invitations: pending team invitations managed by better-auth Organization plugin.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs (
  id          UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id   UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  email       TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  role        TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'viewer'
              ASs="kw">CHECK (role ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'owner', ASs="str">'admASs="kw">IN', ASs="str">'manager', ASs="str">'designer', ASs="str">'bookkeeper', ASs="str">'viewer')),
  ASs="kw">INvited_by  UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  token       TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">UNIQUE,
  status      TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg'
              ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'accepted', ASs="str">'expired', ASs="str">'revoked')),
  expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  accepted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'PASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg team ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs. Token-bASed acceptance flow.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs_email ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs (email);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs_token ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs (token);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs_status ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INvitatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, status);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: AGENTS & EXECUTION
-- =============================================================================

-- Agents: active agent instances per tenant, instantiated from templates.
-- Each tenant gets a configurable set (up to 7 in the Slate template).
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE agents (
  id             UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id      UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  agent_type     TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL
                 ASs="kw">CHECK (agent_type ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'discovery_cASs="kw">ONcierge', ASs="str">'design_spec_assistant', ASs="str">'estimate_engASs="kw">INe', ASs="str">'project_orchestratASs="kw">OR', ASs="str">'ASs="kw">ONperatiASs="kw">ONs_cASs="kw">ONtroller', ASs="str">'executive_navigatASs="kw">OR', ASs="str">'suppASs="kw">ORt_agent')),
  display_name   TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  status         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'active'
                 ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'active', ASs="str">'paused', ASs="str">'disabled')),
  model_tier     TEXT,                        -- e.g., ASs="str">'nano', ASs="str">'haiku', ASs="str">'sASs="kw">ONnet'
  agent_cASs="kw">ONfig   JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'{}', -- tenant-specific overrides
  prompt_versiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INTEGER ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 1,  -- FK to agent_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs.versiASs="kw">ON
  created_at     TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at     TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE agents ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE agents ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON agents
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE agents IS ASs="str">'Active agent ASs="kw">INstances per tenant. ASs="kw">INstantiated from agent templates.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agents_tenant ASs="kw">ON agents (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agents_tenant_type ASs="kw">ON agents (tenant_id, agent_type);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agents_tenant_status ASs="kw">ON agents (tenant_id, status);


-- Agent executions: every agent run is logged here with full input/output,
-- cost tracking, and status lifecycle. This is the core audit trail for
-- all AI activity. Status transitions are defined in Section 7b of the plan.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (
  id              UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id       UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  agent_id        UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES agents(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  trigger_type    TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,               -- e.g., ASs="str">'webhook', ASs="str">'crASs="kw">ON', ASs="str">'manual', ASs="str">'event_bus'
  status          TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'queued'
                  ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (
                    ASs="str">'queued', ASs="str">'runnASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'completed', ASs="str">'failed', ASs="str">'timed_out',
                    ASs="str">'awaitASs="kw">INg_approval', ASs="str">'rejected', ASs="str">'cancelled', ASs="str">'dead_letter'
                  )),
  ASs="kw">INput_cASs="kw">ONtext   JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'{}',
  output          JSASs="kw">ONB,
  tokens_used     ASs="kw">INTEGER,
  cost_usd        NUMERIC(10, 6),
  model_used      TEXT,
  started_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  completed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  errASs="kw">OR_message   TEXT,
  bullmq_job_id   TEXT,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at      TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE agent_executiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE agent_executiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE agent_executiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'Full run histASs="kw">ORy fASs="kw">OR every agent executiASs="kw">ON. CASs="kw">ORe audit trail fASs="kw">OR AI activity.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agent_executiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agent_executiASs="kw">ONs_status ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agent_executiASs="kw">ONs_agent ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, agent_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agent_executiASs="kw">ONs_created ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, created_at DESC);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_agent_executiASs="kw">ONs_bullmq ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs (bullmq_job_id) ASs="kw">WHERE bullmq_job_id IS ASs="kw">NOT NULL;


-- Agent prompt versions: platform-scoped prompt history per agent type.
-- Supports canary rollout (10% → 90%) with automated rollback.
-- PLATFORM-SCOPED: No RLS — shared across all tenants.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE agent_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs (
  id           UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  agent_type   TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  versiASs="kw">ON      ASs="kw">INTEGER ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  system_prompt TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  tools        JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'[]',    -- tool defASs="kw">INitiASs="kw">ONs fASs="kw">OR this versiASs="kw">ON
  model_cASs="kw">ONfig JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'{}',    -- model, max_tokens, temperature, etc.
  is_active    BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false,
  created_by   UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (agent_type, versiASs="kw">ON)
);

-- NO RLS — platform-scoped table, accessible only via service role.
COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE agent_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'PlatfASs="kw">ORm-scoped prompt versiASs="kw">ON histASs="kw">ORy. No RLS — service-role access ASs="kw">ONly.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs_type_active ASs="kw">ON agent_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs (agent_type, is_active) ASs="kw">WHERE is_active = true;
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs_type_versiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON agent_prompt_versiASs="kw">ONs (agent_type, versiASs="kw">ON DESC);


-- Conversation turns: AI conversation history per tenant per agent.
-- Stores the full message thread including tool calls and results.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns (
  id           UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id    UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  agent_id     UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES agents(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  sessiASs="kw">ON_id   UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL,                  -- groups turns ASs="kw">IN a sASs="kw">INgle cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON
  role         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL
               ASs="kw">CHECK (role ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'user', ASs="str">'ASsistant', ASs="str">'system', ASs="str">'tool')),
  cASs="kw">ONtent      TEXT,
  tool_calls   JSASs="kw">ONB,                          -- outbound tool ASs="kw">INvocatiASs="kw">ONs
  tool_results JSASs="kw">ONB,                          -- tool executiASs="kw">ON results
  tokens_used  ASs="kw">INTEGER,
  model_used   TEXT,
  created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns IS ASs="str">'AI cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON histASs="kw">ORy per tenant. Full message thread with tool calls.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns_tenant ASs="kw">ON cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns_sessiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns (tenant_id, sessiASs="kw">ON_id, created_at);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns_agent ASs="kw">ON cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_turns (tenant_id, agent_id);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: HUMAN-IN-THE-LOOP (ESCALATIONS & DRAFTS)
-- =============================================================================

-- Escalations: human-in-the-loop approval queue. Agents escalate decisions
-- that require human review (high-value estimates, risky actions, etc.).
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE escalatiASs="kw">ONs (
  id                  UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id           UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  agent_executiASs="kw">ON_id  UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES agent_executiASs="kw">ONs(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  escalatiASs="kw">ON_type     TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,           -- e.g., ASs="str">'high_value_estimate', ASs="str">'risky_actiASs="kw">ON', ASs="str">'low_cASs="kw">ONfidence'
  priASs="kw">ORity            TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'medium'
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (priASs="kw">ORity ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'low', ASs="str">'medium', ASs="str">'high', ASs="str">'critical')),
  status              TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg'
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'approved', ASs="str">'rejected', ASs="str">'expired')),
  title               TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  summary             TEXT,
  ASs="kw">ORigASs="kw">INal_output     JSASs="kw">ONB,                   -- agent's proposed output
  modified_output     JSASs="kw">ONB,                   -- human-modified versiASs="kw">ON (if edited befASs="kw">ORe approval)
  ASsigned_to         UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  resolved_by         UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  resolved_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  expires_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE escalatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE escalatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE escalatiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'Human-ASs="kw">IN-the-loop approval queue fASs="kw">OR agent decisiASs="kw">ONs requirASs="kw">INg review.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_escalatiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_escalatiASs="kw">ONs_status ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_escalatiASs="kw">ONs_ASsigned ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, ASsigned_to) ASs="kw">WHERE ASsigned_to IS ASs="kw">NOT NULL;
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_escalatiASs="kw">ONs_executiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs (agent_executiASs="kw">ON_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_escalatiASs="kw">ONs_expires ASs="kw">ON escalatiASs="kw">ONs (expires_at) ASs="kw">WHERE status = ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg';


-- Drafts: agent-generated content pending human approval.
-- Covers email drafts, estimates, reports, and recommended actions.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE drafts (
  id                  UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id           UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  agent_executiASs="kw">ON_id  UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES agent_executiASs="kw">ONs(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  draft_type          TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (draft_type ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'email', ASs="str">'estimate', ASs="str">'repASs="kw">ORt', ASs="str">'actiASs="kw">ON')),
  status              TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg'
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'approved', ASs="str">'rejected', ASs="str">'edited')),
  cASs="kw">ONtent             JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL,          -- current cASs="kw">ONtent (may be human-edited)
  ASs="kw">ORigASs="kw">INal_cASs="kw">ONtent    JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL,          -- ASs="kw">ORigASs="kw">INal agent-generated cASs="kw">ONtent
  approved_by         UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id),
  approved_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE drafts ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE drafts ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON drafts
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE drafts IS ASs="str">'Agent-generated drafts (emails, estimates, repASs="kw">ORts) pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg human approval.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_drafts_tenant ASs="kw">ON drafts (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_drafts_status ASs="kw">ON drafts (tenant_id, status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_drafts_type ASs="kw">ON drafts (tenant_id, draft_type);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_drafts_executiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON drafts (agent_executiASs="kw">ON_id);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: EVENT BUS & WEBHOOKS
-- =============================================================================

-- Event bus log: full audit trail of all NATS JetStream messages.
-- Every inter-agent event is logged for debugging and compliance.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE event_bus_log (
  id            UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id     UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  subject       TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,                 -- NATS subject, e.g., ASs="str">'TENANT_{tenantId}.agent.estimate.executiASs="kw">ON.completed'
  payload       JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  source_agent  TEXT,                          -- agent_type of the publisher
  target_agent  TEXT,                          -- agent_type of the ASs="kw">INtASs="kw">ENDed cASs="kw">ONsumer
  status        TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'published'
                ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'published', ASs="str">'delivered', ASs="str">'failed')),
  nats_sequence BIGASs="kw">INT,                        -- NATS JetStream sequence number
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE event_bus_log ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE event_bus_log ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON event_bus_log
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE event_bus_log IS ASs="str">'Full audit trail of NATS JetStream ASs="kw">INter-agent events.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_event_bus_tenant ASs="kw">ON event_bus_log (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_event_bus_tenant_created ASs="kw">ON event_bus_log (tenant_id, created_at DESC);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_event_bus_subject ASs="kw">ON event_bus_log (tenant_id, subject);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_event_bus_status ASs="kw">ON event_bus_log (tenant_id, status) ASs="kw">WHERE status = ASs="str">'failed';


-- Webhook events: deduplication and audit trail for inbound webhooks.
-- Uses (provider, external_event_id, tenant_id) unique constraint for
-- at-least-once deduplication via INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE webhook_events (
  id                UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id         UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  provider          TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,             -- e.g., ASs="str">'jobtread', ASs="str">'quickbooks', ASs="str">'sASs="kw">ENDgrid'
  external_event_id TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,             -- provider's event ID fASs="kw">OR dedup
  event_type        TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,             -- e.g., ASs="str">'ASs="kw">INvoice.created', ASs="str">'project.ASs="kw">UPDATEd'
  payload           JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  status            TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'received'
                    ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'received', ASs="str">'processASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'processed', ASs="str">'failed')),
  idempotency_key   TEXT,
  processed_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (provider, external_event_id, tenant_id)
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE webhook_events ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE webhook_events ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON webhook_events
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE webhook_events IS ASs="str">'ASs="kw">INbound webhook deduplicatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">AND audit. 7-day retentiASs="kw">ON TTL.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_webhook_events_tenant ASs="kw">ON webhook_events (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_webhook_events_dedup ASs="kw">ON webhook_events (provider, external_event_id, tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_webhook_events_status ASs="kw">ON webhook_events (tenant_id, status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_webhook_events_created ASs="kw">ON webhook_events (created_at);  -- fASs="kw">OR TTL cleanup


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: INTEGRATIONS
-- =============================================================================

-- Integrations: OAuth connections to external services.
-- Tokens are encrypted with AES-256-GCM using tenant-specific DEKs (see tenant_keys).
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs (
  id                      UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id               UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  provider                TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (provider ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'jobtread', ASs="str">'quickbooks', ASs="str">'google_calASs="kw">ENDar', ASs="str">'gmail', ASs="str">'outlook_email', ASs="str">'outlook_calASs="kw">ENDar')),
  status                  TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg'
                          ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'cASs="kw">ONnected', ASs="str">'discASs="kw">ONnected', ASs="str">'errASs="kw">OR', ASs="str">'pASs="kw">ENDASs="kw">INg')),
  oauth_tokens_encrypted  BYTEA,               -- AES-256-GCM encrypted OAuth tokens
  token_expires_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  lASt_sync_at            TIMESTAMPTZ,
  sync_cASs="kw">ONfig             JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'{}',  -- provider-specific sync ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INgs
  created_at              TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at              TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (tenant_id, provider)
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'OAuth cASs="kw">ONnectiASs="kw">ONs to external services. Tokens encrypted with tenant DEKs.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs_tenant_provider ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, provider);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs_status ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, status);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs_token_expiry ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs (token_expires_at) ASs="kw">WHERE status = ASs="str">'cASs="kw">ONnected';


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: SUPPORT AGENT
-- =============================================================================

-- Support conversations: chat sessions between users and the Support Agent.
-- Scoped to tenant + user for "my conversations" views.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs (
  id                  UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id           UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id             UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  status              TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'active'
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (status ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'active', ASs="str">'resolved', ASs="str">'escalated')),
  channel             TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'chat'
                      ASs="kw">CHECK (channel ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'chat', ASs="str">'email', ASs="str">'proactive')),
  satisfactiASs="kw">ON_ratASs="kw">INg ASs="kw">INTEGER ASs="kw">CHECK (satisfactiASs="kw">ON_ratASs="kw">INg BETWEEN 1 ASs="kw">AND 5),
  created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  resolved_at         TIMESTAMPTZ
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'SuppASs="kw">ORt Agent chat sessiASs="kw">ONs. Scoped to tenant + user.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs_user ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, user_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs_status ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, status);


-- Support messages: individual messages within a support conversation.
-- Includes agent confidence scores and KB citations for transparency.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_messages (
  id              UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_id UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES suppASs="kw">ORt_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  tenant_id       UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  role            TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL
                  ASs="kw">CHECK (role ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'user', ASs="str">'agent', ASs="str">'system')),
  cASs="kw">ONtent         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  citatiASs="kw">ONs       JSASs="kw">ONB,                       -- KB article ASs="kw">REFERENCES [{article_id, title, chunk}]
  cASs="kw">ONfidence      NUMERIC(3, 2),               -- 0.00–1.00 retrieval cASs="kw">ONfidence scASs="kw">ORe
  tokens_used     ASs="kw">INTEGER,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_messages ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_messages ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_messages
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE suppASs="kw">ORt_messages IS ASs="str">'ASs="kw">INdividual messages ASs="kw">IN suppASs="kw">ORt cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ONs. ASs="kw">INcludes cASs="kw">ONfidence scASs="kw">ORes ASs="kw">AND KB citatiASs="kw">ONs.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_suppASs="kw">ORt_messages_cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_messages (cASs="kw">ONversatiASs="kw">ON_id, created_at);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_suppASs="kw">ORt_messages_tenant ASs="kw">ON suppASs="kw">ORt_messages (tenant_id);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: BILLING & USAGE
-- =============================================================================

-- Tenant usage daily: pre-aggregated daily usage metrics per tenant per agent type.
-- Powers the CEO dashboard, billing metering, and spending limit enforcement.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE tenant_usage_daily (
  id              UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id       UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  date            DATE ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  agent_type      TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  executiASs="kw">ON_count ASs="kw">INTEGER ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_ASs="kw">INput    BIGASs="kw">INT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_output   BIGASs="kw">INT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 0,
  tokens_cached   BIGASs="kw">INT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 0,
  cost_usd        NUMERIC(10, 6) ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 0,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (tenant_id, date, agent_type)
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE tenant_usage_daily ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE tenant_usage_daily ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON tenant_usage_daily
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE tenant_usage_daily IS ASs="str">'Pre-aggregated daily usage metrics. Powers CEO dAShboard ASs="kw">AND billASs="kw">INg meterASs="kw">INg.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_usage_daily_tenant ASs="kw">ON tenant_usage_daily (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_usage_daily_tenant_date ASs="kw">ON tenant_usage_daily (tenant_id, date DESC);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_usage_daily_date ASs="kw">ON tenant_usage_daily (date);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: NOTIFICATIONS
-- =============================================================================

-- Notifications: in-app notifications delivered via Socket.io.
-- Covers escalation alerts, usage warnings, proactive support, etc.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ONs (
  id         UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id  UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id    UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  type       TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,                    -- e.g., ASs="str">'escalatiASs="kw">ON', ASs="str">'usage_warnASs="kw">INg', ASs="str">'oauth_expiry', ASs="str">'agent_failure'
  title      TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  body       TEXT,
  data       JSASs="kw">ONB,                            -- structured metadata (lASs="kw">INks, actiASs="kw">ON buttASs="kw">ONs, etc.)
  read_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now()
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ONs ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ONs IS ASs="str">'ASs="kw">IN-app notificatiASs="kw">ONs delivered via Socket.io.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_notificatiASs="kw">ONs_tenant ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_notificatiASs="kw">ONs_user ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, user_id, created_at DESC);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_notificatiASs="kw">ONs_unread ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ONs (tenant_id, user_id, read_at) ASs="kw">WHERE read_at IS NULL;


-- Notification preferences: per-user, per-category channel toggles.
-- Controls which notifications go to which channel (in-app, email, digest).
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES (
  id               UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  user_id          UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  tenant_id        UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  categASs="kw">ORy         TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL,              -- e.g., ASs="str">'escalatiASs="kw">ONs', ASs="str">'usage_alerts', ASs="str">'agent_failures', ASs="str">'ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs'
  ASs="kw">IN_app           BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT true,
  email            BOOLEAN ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT false,
  digest_frequency TEXT ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="str">'nASs="kw">ONe'
                   ASs="kw">CHECK (digest_frequency ASs="kw">IN (ASs="str">'realtime', ASs="str">'daily', ASs="str">'weekly', ASs="str">'nASs="kw">ONe')),
  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at       TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (user_id, tenant_id, categASs="kw">ORy)
);

ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES ASs="kw">ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ASs="kw">ALTER TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES ASs="kw">FASs="kw">ORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

ASs="kw">CREATE POLICY tenant_isolatiASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR ALL
  ASs="kw">USASs="kw">ING (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid)
  ASs="kw">WITH ASs="kw">CHECK (tenant_id = (ASs="kw">SELECT ASs="fn">current_ASs="kw">SETtASs="kw">INg(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', true))::uuid);

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES IS ASs="str">'Per-user, per-categASs="kw">ORy notificatiASs="kw">ON channel pASs="kw">REFERENCES.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_notificatiASs="kw">ON_prefs_user ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES (user_id, tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_notificatiASs="kw">ON_prefs_tenant ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES (tenant_id);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: SECURITY & ENCRYPTION
-- =============================================================================

-- Tenant keys: envelope encryption data encryption keys (DEKs).
-- Each tenant has a DEK encrypted by a platform KEK (stored in env vars).
-- Used to encrypt OAuth tokens in the integrations table.
-- PLATFORM-SCOPED: No RLS — service-role access only, never exposed to clients.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE tenant_keys (
  id            UUID ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">gen_rASs="kw">ANDom_uuid(),
  tenant_id     UUID ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">REFERENCES tenants(id) ASs="kw">ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">DELETE CASCADE,
  key_versiASs="kw">ON   ASs="kw">INTEGER ASs="kw">NOT NULL ASs="kw">DEFAULT 1,
  encrypted_dek BYTEA ASs="kw">NOT NULL,                -- AES-256-GCM encrypted DEK
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  rotated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ASs="kw">UNIQUE (tenant_id, key_versiASs="kw">ON)
);

-- NO RLS — service-role access only. Never exposed via client queries.
COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE tenant_keys IS ASs="str">'Envelope encryptiASs="kw">ON DEKs per tenant. Service-role access ASs="kw">ONly — no RLS.';

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenant_keys_tenant ASs="kw">ON tenant_keys (tenant_id);
ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">INDEX idx_tenant_keys_active ASs="kw">ON tenant_keys (tenant_id, key_versiASs="kw">ON DESC);


-- =============================================================================
-- DOMAIN: PLATFORM CONFIGURATION
-- =============================================================================

-- System config: platform-wide key-value configuration.
-- Used for feature flags, rate limit defaults, model routing config, etc.
-- PLATFORM-SCOPED: No RLS — service-role access only.
ASs="kw">CREATE TABLE system_cASs="kw">ONfig (
  key        TEXT ASs="kw">PRIMARY KEY,
  value      JSASs="kw">ONB ASs="kw">NOT NULL,
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at TIMESTAMPTZ ASs="kw">DEFAULT ASs="fn">now(),
  ASs="kw">UPDATEd_by UUID ASs="kw">REFERENCES users(id)
);

-- NO RLS — platform-scoped table, accessible only via service role.
COMMENT ASs="kw">ON TABLE system_cASs="kw">ONfig IS ASs="str">'PlatfASs="kw">ORm-wide key-value cASs="kw">ONfiguratiASs="kw">ON. No RLS — service-role access ASs="kw">ONly.';


-- =============================================================================
-- HELPER FUNCTION: set_tenant_context
-- Called by withTenantContext() wrapper in Hono middleware before every
-- tenant-scoped database operation. Sets the session variable that all
-- RLS policies reference.
-- =============================================================================

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">OR REPLACE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">ASs="kw">SET_tenant_cASs="kw">ONtext(p_tenant_id UUID)
ASs="kw">ASs="kw">RETURNS void
ASs="kw">LANGUAGE plpgsql
ASs="kw">SECURITY DEFASs="kw">INER
ASs="kw">SET search_path = ASs="str">''
AS $$
ASs="kw">BEGASs="kw">IN
  ASs="kw">PERFASs="kw">ORM ASs="fn">ASs="kw">SET_cASs="kw">ONfig(ASs="str">'app.tenant_id', p_tenant_id::text, true);  -- true = local to transactiASs="kw">ON
ASs="kw">END;
$$;

COMMENT ASs="kw">ON FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">ASs="kw">SET_tenant_cASs="kw">ONtext IS ASs="str">'ASs="kw">SETs app.tenant_id fASs="kw">OR RLS policies. Called at the start of every tenant-scoped transactiASs="kw">ON.';


-- =============================================================================
-- HELPER FUNCTION: updated_at trigger
-- Automatically updates the updated_at column on row modification.
-- =============================================================================

ASs="kw">CREATE ASs="kw">OR REPLACE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at()
ASs="kw">ASs="kw">RETURNS TRIGGER
ASs="kw">LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
ASs="kw">BEGASs="kw">IN
  ASs="kw">NEW.ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at = ASs="fn">now();
  ASs="kw">RETURN ASs="kw">NEW;
ASs="kw">END;
$$;

-- Apply updated_at triggers to all tables with an updated_at column
ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON users
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();

ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON tenants
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();

ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON agents
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();

ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON agent_executiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();

ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON ASs="kw">INtegratiASs="kw">ONs
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();

ASs="kw">CREATE TRIGGER ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at ASs="kw">BEFASs="kw">ORE ASs="kw">UPDATE ASs="kw">ON notificatiASs="kw">ON_pASs="kw">REFERENCES
  ASs="kw">FASs="kw">OR EACH ROW ASs="kw">EXECUTE FUNCTIASs="kw">ON ASs="fn">trigger_ASs="kw">SET_ASs="kw">UPDATEd_at();
Migration Strategy

DDL is the source of truth. Drizzle schema files are generated via drizzle-kit introspect:pg. Migrations managed via supabase migration new + supabase db push. Seed data for staging defined in packages/backend/seed/.

6. Evolvability Architecture (Built to Evolve)

The platform must be trivially updatable — new agents, new integrations, new verticals — without rewriting core infrastructure.

Plugin-Based Agent System (Factory + Registration Pattern)

Each agent = self-contained module using a factory + registration pattern (inspired by pi-mono’s extension system). Instead of a monolithic interface, agents receive a registration API and progressively declare their capabilities:

TypeScript
// Factory pattern — agents register capabilities declaratively
type AgentPluginFactory = (api: AgentRegistrationAPI) => void | Promise<void>

interface AgentRegistrationAPI {
  setMetadata(meta: AgentMetadata): void
  registerTool<TParams extends TSchema>(tool: AgentToolDefinition<TParams>): void
  registerTrigger(trigger: TriggerConfig): void
  requireIntegration(id: string): void
  registerDashboardComponent(component: React.LazyExoticComponent<any>): void
  registerEventSubjects(subjects: string[]): void

  // Typed event handlers with before/after pairs
  on(event: 'before_tool_call', handler: AgentEventHandler<BeforeToolCallEvent, BeforeToolCallResult>): void
  on(event: 'after_tool_call', handler: AgentEventHandler<AfterToolCallEvent, AfterToolCallResult>): void
  on(event: 'before_execute', handler: AgentEventHandler<BeforeExecuteEvent, BeforeExecuteResult>): void
  on(event: 'after_execute', handler: AgentEventHandler<AfterExecuteEvent>): void
  on(event: 'escalation', handler: AgentEventHandler<EscalationEvent>): void
  on(event: 'error', handler: AgentEventHandler<AgentErrorEvent>): void
}

interface AgentMetadata {
  id: string
  name: string
  version: string
  description: string
  category: 'sales' | 'operations' | 'finance' | 'support' | 'custom'
}

// Event handler type — sync or async, optional typed result
type AgentEventHandler<E, R = void> =
  (event: E, ctx: AgentExecutionContext) => Promise<R | void> | R | void

// before_tool_call can BLOCK or MODIFY — enables dynamic permission gating
interface BeforeToolCallResult {
  block?: boolean       // reject the tool call
  reason?: string      // rejection reason (logged + surfaced)
  modifiedParams?: Record<string, unknown>
}

// after_tool_call can TRANSFORM output or TRIGGER escalation
interface AfterToolCallResult {
  content?: unknown
  isError?: boolean
  escalate?: EscalationRule
}

Example agent registration (Leads Agent):

TypeScript
const discoveryConcierge: AgentPluginFactory = (api) => {
  api.setMetadata({ id: 'discovery-concierge', name: 'Leads Agent',
    version: '1.0.0', description: 'Lead qualification & engagement', category: 'sales' })

  api.requireIntegration('jobtread')
  api.requireIntegration('calendar')   // google-calendar OR outlook-calendar (provider-agnostic)
  api.requireIntegration('email')      // gmail OR outlook-email (provider-agnostic)

  api.registerTrigger({ type: 'nats', subject: 'TENANT_{id}.lead.new' })
  api.registerTrigger({ type: 'cron', schedule: '0 9 * * 1-5', action: 'check_stale_leads' })

  api.registerTool(QueryJobTreadProjects)  // TypeBox-typed tools
  api.registerTool(DraftEmail)
  api.registerTool(CheckCalendarAvailability)

  api.on('before_tool_call', async (event, ctx) => {
    // Dynamic gate: block email drafts if tenant over monthly limit
    if (event.toolName === 'draft_email') {
      const usage = await checkTenantUsage(ctx.tenantId)
      if (usage.emailsThisMonth >= usage.plan.monthlyLimit)
        return { block: true, reason: 'Monthly email limit reached' }
    }
  })

  api.registerDashboardComponent(React.lazy(() => import('./dashboard/DiscoveryConciergePanel')))
}
  • Adding a new agent = implement the factory + register in template
  • No changes to core event bus, executor, or dashboard required
  • Dashboard extensibility: Agents register a lazy-loaded React component via api.registerDashboardComponent(). Agents without a custom component get a generic status/history fallback. This allows new agents to ship custom UI without modifying the dashboard shell.
  • Event-based permission gating: The before_tool_call handler enables dynamic, contextual permission decisions at runtime — complementing the static permission matrix. Handlers can block tool calls based on usage limits, tenant configuration, or real-time business rules. All handler exceptions are caught and reported without crashing the agent (error isolation).
  • Queue-and-flush registration: During factory execution, registrations are queued. Once the executor runtime binds (via bindCore()), all queued registrations flush to live systems (NATS subjects, BullMQ triggers, tool registry). Post-bind registrations take effect immediately. This prevents race conditions during agent initialization.
  • Provenance: Factory + registration pattern, typed event handler chain, error isolation, and promptGuidelines on tools adopted from analysis of badlogic/pi-mono’s 47KB extension type system (pi-mono is an AI coding agent toolkit by the creator of libGDX). Coding-agent-specific patterns (CLI commands, terminal UI, session branching, provider registration) were excluded as irrelevant to Kiptra’s web-based SaaS architecture.

Schema Evolution

  • Supabase migrations for schema changes (version-controlled)
  • JSONB columns for agent-specific data (avoids rigid schema per agent type)
  • agent_config and agent_output as JSONB — flexible without migrations
JSONB Governance (Mandatory)

Every JSONB column MUST have: (1) a Zod schema defining its structure, (2) GIN indexes on queried paths, (3) a config_version integer column alongside it for schema evolution, (4) a background migration job that upgrades old versions to current, (5) all supabase-js queries on JSONB columns use .returns<T>() for type safety. Unvalidated JSONB is a data integrity risk — treat every JSONB column as a typed document store.

Feature Flags

  • Per-tenant feature flags (enable beta agents for specific customers)
  • Gradual rollouts (10% → 50% → 100%)
  • Kill switch for any agent/feature without deployment
  • Phase 1 implementation (< 100 tenants): Feature flags stored as a feature_flags JSONB column on the tenants table. A getFeatureFlag(tenantId, flagName) function queries this column. No external service dependency. Graduate to Unleash or PostHog when non-engineer flag management is needed.
  • Feature flag caching: Valkey cache with 60-second TTL per tenant (tenant:{id}:feature_flags). Cache is invalidated via a NATS event (TENANT_{id}.config.feature_flags.updated) published on any flag update, ensuring near-instant propagation without polling.

Integration Registry

New integrations = implement adapter interface + register:

TypeScript
// Canonical domain model types defined in src/domain/
// (Project, Estimate, Contact, etc.)

interface IntegrationAdapter<TReadResult, TWritePayload> {
  id: string
  authMethod: 'api_key' | 'oauth2'
  connect(tenantId: string): Promise<void>
  disconnect(tenantId: string): Promise<void>
  read(endpoint: string, params: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<TReadResult>
  write?(endpoint: string, data: TWritePayload): Promise<TReadResult>  // optional
}

Type safety: The adapter is generic: IntegrationAdapter<TReadResult, TWritePayload> with read() returning Promise<TReadResult> and write() accepting TWritePayload. The canonical domain model types (Project, Estimate, Contact, etc. defined in src/domain/) should be the constraint. This ensures that swapping JobTread for Procore is verified at compile time, not just at runtime.

  • Adding JobTread support = 1 adapter file. Adding a new CRM = 1 adapter file.
  • Adding a new integration adapter requires writing a TypeScript file implementing the standard interface and a standard deployment (under 5 minutes via CI/CD pipeline). In development mode, a file watcher enables hot-reload for rapid iteration.
  • Adapter registration: A central registry.ts exports registerAdapter(adapter: IntegrationAdapter) backed by a Map<string, IntegrationAdapter>. In development, a file watcher auto-registers new adapter files. In production, static imports in the registry module ensure tree-shaking and startup-time validation. The registry provides getAdapter(id) and listAdapters() for runtime discovery.

Versioned Agent Templates

  • Templates are versioned (v1, v2, v3...)
  • Customers can be on different template versions
  • Upgrade path: notify → preview changes → confirm → migrate
  • Prompt versioning: Each agent’s agent_config includes prompt_version and prompt_hash fields. An agent_prompt_versions table stores all historical prompt versions per agent type. Prompt updates follow staged canary rollout: deploy to 10% of tenants first, monitor quality metrics for 48 hours, then roll to 90%. Rollback = update prompt_version to previous hash. Pre-Wave-3 rollback: Manual via prompt_version update. Automated canary rollback available Wave 3+.
  • Rollback criteria: If approval rate drops >15% relative to baseline during the 48-hour canary window, auto-rollback. If escalation rate increases >2x, auto-rollback. The 10% canary cohort is selected via the feature flag JSONB column. Note: Meaningful quality metrics require Wave 3 AI quality layer — canary rollout with automated rollback is a Wave 3+ capability.

How New Verticals and Personas Are Added

Every new vertical follows a template-based approach. The platform ships with a plugin architecture where industry knowledge lives entirely in agent prompts and JSONB configuration — not in the platform code itself. Adding a vertical means creating a template row, configuring agent prompts, and optionally writing new agent/integration plugins. Adding a new persona within an existing vertical (e.g., adding “dealer” to Kitchen & Bath) follows the same pattern: create template configurations (prompt templates, dashboard layouts, onboarding content, tour definitions). No code changes required. The platform is industry-agnostic and persona-agnostic; all domain knowledge lives in templates. See Section 6a for the full multi-persona architecture.

VerticalReused AgentsNew/Modified AgentsIntegration SwapsTemplate Config
Design-Build All 6 (reconfigured prompts) None — same agents, different JSONB config Same integrations Different qualification criteria, estimate categories, Drive folder hierarchy
HVAC Service Leads Agent, Sales Agent, Bookkeeping Agent, CEO Agent Equipment Spec Tracker (replaces Design Agent), Service Route Optimizer (replaces Project Management Agent) ServiceTitan replaces JobTread Dispatch-focused triggers, equipment warranty tracking
Landscaping Leads Agent, Sales Agent, CEO Agent Seasonal Planning Agent (new) Weather API more critical, simplified project management Seasonal scheduling, material estimation by season
General Contracting All 6 Permit Tracker (new addition) Same + permit tracking API Permit milestone triggers, multi-project coordination
Business OS CEO Agent (dashboard data), Bookkeeping Agent (meeting actions) 7 new Business OS-specific agents (Business OS Agent, Meeting Facilitator, Goal & Alignment, KPI/Scorecard, People & Review, Knowledge Portal, AI Assistant) Google/Microsoft Calendar OAuth, existing Supabase infra Full business operating system: scorecards, quarterly goals, weekly sync meetings, accountability charts, issue tracking, knowledge base, vision docs
Kitchen & Bath Design Leads Agent (client intake), Sales Agent (BOM pricing) Design Layout Agent (Claude → Z3 → Gemini loop), Catalog Match Agent (pgvector RAG) Manufacturer catalog APIs, Three.js/R3F rendering pipeline Browser-based 3D kitchen/bath design with AI layout generation, NKBA constraint validation, photorealistic rendering, BOM export

Key Insight: The platform doesn’t know what industry it’s serving. It just runs whatever agents the template tells it to run. Industry knowledge lives in agent prompts and configs (JSONB), not in the platform infrastructure.

What Changes vs What Stays

Stays the Same (100% reused across every vertical):

  • Dual VPS + Docker Compose (same infrastructure, same containers)
  • NATS JetStream (same event bus, new subject namespaces auto-created)
  • Valkey + BullMQ (same cache + job queue)
  • Supabase + RLS (same database, same isolation)
  • Vercel AI Gateway (same model routing)
  • Vercel dashboard (same frontend framework)
  • Stripe billing (same payment system)
  • better-auth (same authentication)
  • Approval Queue (same human-in-the-loop)
  • Multi-tenant isolation (same 6 layers)

What You Create (per new vertical):

  • 1 template config row in agent_templates table
  • Agent prompt configs as JSONB (no database migrations)
  • New agent files if needed (1 TypeScript file per agent implementing AgentPluginFactory)
  • New integration adapters if needed (1 TypeScript file implementing IntegrationAdapter)
  • Folder hierarchy template for Google Drive
  • Qualification criteria for Leads Agent

Bottom Line: Adding a new vertical is primarily a configuration exercise, not an engineering project. The first vertical (construction remodeling) requires building the platform. Every subsequent vertical is just template configuration + any genuinely new agent/integration plugins. Qualification: For construction sub-verticals (Design-Build, General Contracting): configuration only. For fundamentally different verticals (HVAC, K&B, Business OS): new agent code and potentially new infrastructure required (e.g., Three.js rendering pipeline for K&B, meeting facilitation workflows for Business OS).

Design-Build Example (Detailed Walkthrough)

Design-build is the first planned expansion after the construction remodeling MVP. Here is exactly what changes — and what doesn’t — when adding this vertical:

  1. Create “Design-Build” template in agent_templates — same 6 agents, different configs. One row in the database, zero schema changes.
  2. Leads Agent: swap qualification criteria in agent_config JSONB — “ideal design-build client” vs “ideal remodel client” is different prompts, not different code.
  3. Design Agent: adjust Drive folder hierarchy — Design Phase → Schematic → Design Development → Construction Documents (instead of Room → Category).
  4. Sales Agent: add design fee categories to estimate config — design-build packages include design fees that pure remodelers don’t charge.
  5. Project Management Agent: adjust schedule templates — design-build has distinct design and construction phases with different milestone structures.
  6. Bookkeeping Agent + CEO Agent: zero changes — billing is billing, CEO briefings aggregate from whatever agents exist.

Total engineering effort for design-build vertical: ~1–2 days of template configuration. Zero code changes to the platform.

Adding verticals that share construction-domain concepts (Design-Build, General Contracting) requires 1–2 days of template configuration. Adding fundamentally different verticals (HVAC, Landscaping) requires 1–2 weeks of new adapter development plus canonical model extensions (new entity types like DispatchJob, Technician, Equipment). This is still a strong advantage over rebuilding from scratch.

6a. Multi-Persona Architecture & Industry/Template Routing

The platform serves multiple business personas within the K&B supply chain through a two-axis template routing system: industry_vertical × tenant_type. Each combination resolves a template that configures the entire tenant experience — dashboard layout, sidebar navigation, agent configuration, Business OS modules, integrations, onboarding checklist, product tour, and RBAC roles. Templates are configuration, not code.

Key Principle

Each business IS one persona type. A dealer is a dealer. A manufacturer is a manufacturer. They are separate tenants with cross-tenant interactions via a B2B network layer — NOT roles within a single tenant. This avoids an unmanageable RBAC matrix and muddled UX. See kiptra-multi-persona-architecture.md for full research (893 lines).

Two-Axis Template Routing

AxisValues (Day 1)Future Values
industry_vertical kitchen_bath hvac, general_construction, landscaping
tenant_type contractor (Wave 1–4) dealer, sales_rep, manufacturer (Wave 5+)

The template applied at onboarding controls:

  • Dashboard layout — persona-specific KPIs, widgets, and data visualization
  • Sidebar navigation — only persona-relevant modules exposed
  • Agent configuration — which agents are instantiated, with persona-tuned prompt templates
  • Business OS modules — full suite for contractors/dealers/manufacturers; lite for sales reps
  • Integrations — persona-relevant integration options
  • Onboarding checklist — persona-specific setup tasks
  • Product tour — persona-adapted tour content and module-specific triggers
  • RBAC roles — persona-appropriate role names and permissions

Templates are customizable after onboarding. The system applies a sensible default; tenants adjust from there.

Onboarding Flow (Updated)

Flow
Sign Up → Company Info → "What industry are you in?" → "What's your role in the supply chain?"
→ Template applied → Plan Selection → Payment → Team Invites → Guided Tour (template-specific)

The industry and persona selections are added to the onboarding wizard after company info and before plan selection. These two selections determine the template. The confirmation screen shows: “Based on your selections, we’ve configured Kiptra for [Industry] [Persona].”

K&B Personas (Day 1 Industry)

PersonaDashboard FocusKey AgentsK&B CanvasUnique Features
Contractor Projects, estimates, scheduling All 7 core Full access Design-to-estimate pipeline
Dealer Orders, contractor accounts, inventory Discovery, Operations, Executive + 2 new (Catalog Pricing, Order Routing) View-only Catalog management, rep management, order routing
Sales Rep Territory, commissions, order status Discovery, Executive + 2 new (Commission Tracker, Territory Intelligence) View-only Territory map, commission tracking
Manufacturer Production, distribution, dealer network Operations, Executive + 3 new (Catalog Publisher, Production Tracker, Dealer Network) No Catalog distribution, production scheduling

Agent Behavior Per Template

Each persona gets 7–9 agents: 7 shared base agents with persona-specific prompt templates, tool permissions, and context injection, plus persona-exclusive agents added as plugins. For example, the Leads Agent finds homeowner leads for contractors but finds contractor leads for dealers. Dealer personas add Catalog Pricing Agent and Order Routing Agent (9 total). Sales Rep personas swap Project Management Agent for Territory Intelligence and Commission Optimizer agents (7 total). Manufacturer personas add Catalog Publisher, Production Scheduler, and Quality Analytics agents (9 total). All persona-specific agents follow the existing AgentPluginFactory pattern without changing core architecture. Total: 28 agent types across 4 personas.

AgentContractor BehaviorDealer BehaviorSales Rep BehaviorManufacturer Behavior
Leads Agent Qualify homeowner leads Qualify contractor leads Track contractor opportunities Qualify dealer applications
Sales Agent Homeowner project estimates Contractor quotes from catalogs N/A Dealer pricing / wholesale quotes
Bookkeeping Agent AP/AR, lien waivers AP/AR, inventory Commission reconciliation AP/AR, production costs
CEO Agent CEO briefing (projects) CEO briefing (sales) Performance summary CEO briefing (production + sales)

Cross-Company B2B Network (Post-Launch)

Tenants can establish bilateral relationships via a B2B network layer. This is NOT a marketplace (Kiptra does not take a cut of transactions). It is a federated network where each tenant operates independently but can connect with other tenants.

  • tenant_relationships table tracks business connections (manufacturer→dealer, dealer→contractor, rep→dealer, etc.)
  • cross_tenant_orders table routes orders between connected tenants
  • shared_catalogs table enables manufacturer catalog distribution to authorized dealers
  • Contractor places order → appears in dealer dashboard → routes to manufacturer — all within Kiptra
  • Cross-tenant events use a separate NATS stream (NETWORK.*) with platform service-role publishing only
  • Security: both tenants must have an active tenant_relationships record before any cross-tenant data access

Pricing: Same Tiers, All Personas

All personas use the same pricing tiers ($275/$500/$750 monthly, $5,000/$10,000/$20,000 setup). The template determines the experience, not the price. Persona-specific pricing (e.g., lower-cost sales rep tier at $99/mo as viral acquisition channel) is evaluated post-launch based on adoption data. See kiptra-multi-persona-architecture.md Section 9 for the full persona-specific pricing analysis when differentiation becomes warranted.

Future Verticals (Template-Only Expansion)

Adding a new industry vertical = creating template configurations (prompt templates, dashboard layouts, onboarding content, tour definitions). No code changes required. The platform is industry-agnostic; industry knowledge lives in templates. Each vertical supports the same 4 persona types with different agent prompts, tools, and Business OS configurations:

  • HVAC: Same 4 persona types, different agents/prompts/tools (dispatch jobs, technician scheduling, equipment tracking)
  • General Construction: Same pattern (bid management, subcontractor coordination, permit tracking)
  • Landscaping: Same pattern (seasonal scheduling, maintenance contracts, material estimation)

Build order: Only the contractor persona ships in Wave 1–4. The tenant_type column is added in Wave 1 (defaulting to 'contractor') to avoid data migration later. Other personas are template additions post-launch, not code changes. The B2B network layer is post-launch, implemented when cross-company ordering demand materializes. Each new persona is ~1–2 weeks of template creation + testing.

7. Integration Architecture (Email, Webhooks, OAuth)

Inbound Email Flow (Lead Capture)

Flow
