TipTap wraps ProseMirror; Hocuspocus implements the prosemirror-collab protocol for real-time collaboration. Server maintains the authoritative document version; clients send steps (operations) and receive rebased confirmations.
  • Offline support: IndexedDB queue for pending mutations. On reconnect, replay queue with conflict detection. Conflicts surface to user for manual resolution (rare edge case).
  • Presence indicators: Avatar badges on records currently being viewed/edited by other users. Typing indicators on text fields.
TypeScript
// Real-time subscription for KPI Dashboard
const channel = supabase
  .channel('kpi-dashboard')
  .on('postgres_changes', {
    event: 'UPDATE',
    schema: 'public',
    table: 'measurable_values',
    filter: `scorecard_id=eq.${scorecardId}`,
  }, (payload) => {
    updateMeasurableValue(payload.new);
  })
  .on('presence', { event: 'sync' }, () => {
    const state = channel.presenceState();
    setActiveEditors(Object.values(state).flat());
  })
  .subscribe();
Architecture Decision: Employee Performance Data RLS & Access Control

Decision: RLS policies on reviews, review_responses, fit_checks, and assessment_responses enforce tenant isolation ONLY (consistent with Section 5’s rule). All role-based visibility logic (who sees what within a tenant) is enforced at the Hono middleware layer.

Key access rules (Hono middleware):

  • Reviews: Reviewee sees own review. Reviewer sees assigned reviews. Owner/admin sees all. Self-assessment and manager review are hidden from each other until both submit (status ≥ meeting_ready).
  • Fit Checks: One-way assessment. The assessed person NEVER sees individual fit check records. They see aggregated trends (“improving” / “stable” / “declining”) computed server-side via a separate API endpoint.
  • Assessment Responses: Anonymity enforced at API layer. Results endpoint returns aggregates only, never user_id. Anonymity threshold: results suppressed when < 5 responses to prevent de-anonymization in small teams. user_id stored for deduplication only.

RLS pattern: All four tables use current_tenant_id() (a STABLE SQL function wrapping current_setting('app.tenant_id', true) in a subquery for initPlan optimization). Tables without tenant_id columns (review_responses, assessment_responses) use EXISTS joins to their parent table.

Indexes: Composite indexes on (tenant_id, status), (tenant_id, reviewee_id), (tenant_id, assessor_id), (tenant_id, year, quarter). No index on assessment_responses.user_id — intentional, to discourage per-user queries and reinforce anonymity at the data layer.

Architecture Decision: TipTap Everywhere + Hocuspocus

Decision: Use TipTap as the sole rich-text editor across all Business OS modules (vision plan narratives, meeting notes, Knowledge Portal articles). Collaborative editing powered by Hocuspocus (self-hosted, MIT license).

Rationale: Section 15.4.7 already specifies TipTap for Knowledge Portal. Section 15.4.1 specifies prosemirror-collab for collaborative text — Hocuspocus IS a production-grade prosemirror-collab authority server with built-in WebSocket transport, awareness (cursors/selections), offline reconnection, and persistence hooks. Using TipTap everywhere eliminates a second editor codebase and leverages 50+ ready-made extensions for tables, images, embeds, checklists, callouts, and code blocks.

Architecture:

  • Shared packages/editor/ workspace with TipTap core + extensions
  • Hocuspocus deployed as Node.js sidecar alongside Hono API
  • Persistence: onStoreDocument hook writes to Supabase JSONB columns
  • Auth: onAuthenticate hook validates existing JWT
  • Presence: Hocuspocus awareness protocol (no Supabase Realtime connection cap concern)
  • Cost: $0 (both MIT-licensed, self-hosted)
  • Bundle overhead vs raw ProseMirror: ~40–60KB gzipped — acceptable given extension savings

Clarification on Section 15.4.1: The plan’s reference to “ProseMirror + prosemirror-collab” is not contradicted — TipTap uses ProseMirror underneath, and Hocuspocus implements the prosemirror-collab protocol. This decision standardizes the wrapper layer.

15.4.2 Workflow Engine (XState v5)

Several Business OS modules have complex lifecycle workflows that must be modeled as finite state machines: meeting progression through timed sections, quarterly goal lifecycle, review approval chains, and issue resolution. XState v5 provides a production-grade state machine library with persistence, visualization, and testing support.

State machines required:

Machine States Key Transitions
Meeting Lifecycle scheduled → in_progress → section_active → paused → wrapping_up → completed Timer-driven section transitions, manual override, early end
Quarterly Goal Lifecycle draft → active → on_track / off_track / at_risk → completed / dropped Status changes, milestone completion, quarter-end evaluation
Review Workflow not_started → self_assessment → manager_review → discussion → finalized Submission triggers, manager action, discussion scheduling
Issue Resolution (IRP) identified → prioritized → discussing → resolved → archived Priority ranking, discussion assignment, resolution recording
Approval Chain pending → reviewing → approved / rejected → revision → re_review Multi-level approval, delegation, timeout escalation 
TypeScript
// Meeting lifecycle state machine (XState v5)
import { setup, assign } from 'xstate';

export const meetingMachine = setup({
  types: {} as {
    context: MeetingContext;
    events: MeetingEvent;
  },
  actions: {
    advanceSection: assign(({ context }) => ({
      currentSectionIndex: context.currentSectionIndex + 1,
      sectionStartedAt: new Date(),
    })),
    recordRating: assign(({ context, event }) => ({
      ratings: [...context.ratings, event.rating],
    })),
  },
  guards: {
    hasMoreSections: ({ context }) =>
      context.currentSectionIndex < context.sections.length - 1,
    allRatingsCollected: ({ context }) =>
      context.ratings.length ≥ context.attendees.length,
  },
}).createMachine({
  id: 'meeting',
  initial: 'scheduled',
  context: ({ input }) => ({
    meetingId: input.meetingId,
    sections: input.sections,
    attendees: input.attendees,
    currentSectionIndex: 0,
    sectionStartedAt: null,
    ratings: [],
  }),
  states: {
    scheduled: { on: { START: 'in_progress' } },
    in_progress: {
      initial: 'section_active',
      states: {
        section_active: {
          after: {
            sectionDuration: [
              { guard: 'hasMoreSections', actions: 'advanceSection', target: 'section_active' },
              { target: '#meeting.wrapping_up' },
            ],
          },
          on: { NEXT_SECTION: [
            { guard: 'hasMoreSections', actions: 'advanceSection' },
            { target: '#meeting.wrapping_up' },
          ]},
        },
      },
      on: { PAUSE: 'paused', END_EARLY: 'wrapping_up' },
    },
    paused: { on: { RESUME: 'in_progress' } },
    wrapping_up: {
      on: {
        RATE: { actions: 'recordRating' },
        COMPLETE: { guard: 'allRatingsCollected', target: 'completed' },
      },
    },
    completed: { type: 'final' },
  },
});

Persistence: Machine state is serialized to a workflow_state JSONB column on the parent record. On server restart or reconnection, the machine is rehydrated from persisted state. BullMQ handles delayed transitions (timer-based section advances) with durable job scheduling.

Architecture Decision: XState v5 Hybrid Execution (Server-Authoritative Workflows)

Decision: All five named state machines (Meeting Lifecycle, Quarterly Goal Lifecycle, Review Workflow, Issue Resolution, Approval Chain) execute on the server (Hono API). Client-side execution is limited to ephemeral UI state (section countdown timers, panel toggles, drag state).

Why not client-only: Meetings have multiple concurrent users driving the same machine. Browser tabs close mid-meeting. Browser timers are throttled in background tabs. Client-side execution creates split-brain risk with no single source of truth.

Why not server-only: Timer countdowns and UI micro-state would require API round-trips for every tick, adding latency that degrades UX.

Architecture:

  • Event flow: Client sends events via POST /api/{entity}/{id}/events. Hono routes to XState machine. Machine transitions, persists new workflow_state JSONB, schedules BullMQ delayed jobs for timeouts.
  • Broadcast: New state broadcast to all connected clients via Supabase Realtime Broadcast channel. Clients render server state as source of truth.
  • Machine registry: LRU cache of active machine instances in Hono process. Machines loaded from DB on first event, evicted after 30 minutes of inactivity.
  • Horizontal scaling: Machine events routed through NATS JetStream (machine.{type}.{id}) with single consumer per machine ID. Prevents concurrent processing across 2 VPS nodes.
  • Meeting timers: Server schedules BullMQ delayed job per section duration. Job fires SECTION_TIMEOUT event to machine via NATS. Durable across server restarts (Valkey-backed). Client shows local countdown from section_started_at + duration_minutes, synced on reconnect.
  • Reconnection: Client fetches current workflow_state from API on reconnect. No client-side machine rehydration — client is a pure state renderer.

15.4.3 Analytics & Visualization

The Analytics Dashboard and KPI Dashboard require rich, interactive data visualization with drill-down capabilities, cross-filtering, and responsive layouts.

  • Chart library: Recharts for all charts (line, bar, area, combo, sparklines) + shadcn/ui Card components for KPI card layouts
  • Data layer: Postgres materialized views for pre-computed aggregates (goal completion rates, KPI trends, meeting effectiveness). Refreshed via BullMQ cron jobs (every 15 minutes for active data, hourly for historical).
  • Caching: Valkey caching for dashboard queries with 5-minute TTL. Cache invalidation on write events via Postgres NOTIFY.
  • Layout: React Grid Layout for user-customizable dashboard arrangements. Layout persisted per-user in user_workspace_layout table.
  • Drill-down: Click any aggregate to see constituent records. KPI off-track → show weekly values → show individual data points. Goal completion rate → show individual goals → show milestones.
  • Cross-filtering: Select a team in one widget, all other widgets filter to that team. Time range selector affects all charts simultaneously.
Architecture Decision: Recharts Only — Drop Tremor (ADR-CHART-001)

Decision: Option A — Recharts only. Remove Tremor from the dependency list. Build KPI cards and sparklines with Recharts + shadcn/ui Card components.

Rationale:

  • Tremor IS Recharts. Tremor v3 (and Tremor Raw) uses Recharts as its internal charting engine. Adding Tremor on top of Recharts means shipping Recharts twice — once directly and once bundled inside Tremor. The “~30 KB gzipped” estimate for Tremor is misleading because it excludes the Recharts dependency it carries.
  • Redundant abstraction layer. Tremor wraps Recharts charts in opinionated Tailwind components (cards, KPI indicators, sparklines). Kiptra already uses shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS as its component layer (Section 3). Building a <KpiCard> component with a shadcn Card + a Recharts <Sparkline /> takes ~40 lines of code and matches the existing design system. Tremor introduces a second component API convention that diverges from shadcn patterns.
  • Bundle savings: Removing Tremor saves ~30 KB gzipped of wrapper code (the underlying Recharts was already included). More importantly, it eliminates a second charting API surface — developers do not need to learn both <AreaChart> from Recharts and <AreaChart> from Tremor, which have different prop signatures.
  • Sparklines in Recharts: Recharts supports sparklines natively via <LineChart> with width={120} height={40}, no axes, no grid, no legend — this is a 10-line component. No library needed for this pattern.
  • Maintenance: One charting library = one upgrade path. Tremor v3 is actively maintained by Vercel (acquired Jan 2025), but pinning to an intermediate abstraction means waiting for Tremor to bump its Recharts dependency before Kiptra can adopt Recharts fixes.

What to build instead of Tremor components:

  • <KpiCard> — shadcn/ui <Card> + Recharts <LineChart> sparkline + delta indicator (green/red arrow with percentage)
  • <MetricSummary> — shadcn/ui <Card> with large number, label, and optional trend line
  • <StatusIndicator> — Tailwind-styled badge (already available via shadcn/ui <Badge>)

Updated dependency table (Section 15.6): Remove the Tremor row. Recharts row updated to: “Interactive charts, sparklines, KPI trends, and analytics — ~45 KB gzipped.”

Updated Section 15.4.3: Replace Tremor for dashboard components (cards, KPI indicators, sparklines) + Recharts for custom charts with Recharts for all charts (line, bar, area, combo, sparklines) + shadcn/ui Card components for KPI card layouts.

15.4.4 Calendar Sync

Meeting Hub requires two-way calendar synchronization so that Weekly Syncs, Quarterly Reviews, and ad-hoc meetings appear on users’ Google Calendar and/or Microsoft Outlook calendars.

  • Google Calendar: Google Calendar API v3 via OAuth2. Scopes: calendar.events (read/write). Incremental sync via syncToken for efficient polling.
  • Microsoft Outlook: Microsoft Graph Calendar API via MSAL (Microsoft Authentication Library). Delta queries for incremental sync.
  • Recurring events: RFC 5545 RRULE support for recurring meetings (weekly syncs are always recurring). Store RRULE in DB; generate occurrences on read.
  • Sync direction: Two-way. Meeting created in Kiptra → event created in external calendar. Meeting rescheduled in external calendar → Kiptra meeting updated. Conflict resolution: Kiptra is source of truth for meeting content; external calendar is source of truth for time changes.
  • Sync jobs: BullMQ repeatable jobs per user/calendar connection. Poll interval: 5 minutes. Webhook-based push notifications where supported (Google Push Notifications, Microsoft Graph subscriptions).

15.4.5 Notification System

Business OS modules generate a high volume of notifications: meeting reminders, action item due dates, KPI off-track alerts, goal status changes, review assignments, and issue escalations. The notification system must be channel-agnostic, user-configurable, and deduplication-aware.

  • Event-driven architecture: All Business OS events publish to NATS JetStream. Notification service subscribes to relevant subjects and generates notification records.
  • Channel router: Each notification is routed to one or more channels based on user preferences:
    • In-app: Socket.io push to connected clients. Stored in notifications table with read/unread status.
    • Email: SendGrid transactional emails with customizable templates. Batched digest option (daily/weekly).
    • Push: Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) for mobile/desktop push notifications.
  • User preferences: Per-notification-type, per-channel toggles. Example: “KPI off-track” → in-app + email; “Meeting starting” → in-app + push; “Announcement posted” → in-app only.
  • Deduplication: Hash-based dedup within a 5-minute window. Prevents duplicate notifications from retry logic or event replays.
  • Rate limiting: Maximum 10 notifications per user per hour per channel. Excess notifications batched into a digest.
  • Digest scheduling: Users can opt into daily or weekly email digests instead of individual notifications. BullMQ cron job aggregates and sends.

15.4.6 PDF & Report Generation

Several modules require PDF export: meeting recaps, vision plan documents, quarterly goal reports, KPI dashboard snapshots, and review summaries.

  • Structured reports: React-PDF (@react-pdf/renderer) for programmatic PDF generation. React components render directly to PDF with full layout control. Used for meeting recaps, goal reports, and review documents.
  • Complex visual reports: Puppeteer for rendering HTML/CSS to PDF when charts and complex layouts are needed (KPI dashboard snapshots, analytics reports). Headless Chrome in a Docker sidecar container.
  • Async generation: All PDF generation runs as BullMQ jobs to avoid blocking API requests. Job creates PDF, uploads to object storage (S3-compatible), and notifies user via the notification system.
  • Templates: Meeting recap, Strategic Vision Plan export, Quarterly Goal report, KPI Dashboard snapshot, Review summary, Responsibility Map org chart.

15.4.7 Knowledge Portal

The Knowledge Portal is the most content-heavy module — a combined learning management system and internal wiki. Its editor and content model require special attention.

  • Editor: TipTap (ProseMirror-based) with extensions for tables, images, embeds, checklists, callouts, and code blocks. Collaborative editing via prosemirror-collab (same infrastructure as vision plan text).
  • Content model: Articles are composed of ContentBlock records (JSONB) supporting text, image, video, embed, checklist, and quiz block types. Blocks are ordered and independently versioned.
  • Search: Postgres tsvector for full-text search across article content + pg_trgm for fuzzy/partial matching. Search results ranked by relevance with snippet highlighting.
  • Assignment & tracking: Seat-based assignment (assign articles/collections to seats, not people — consistent with Responsibility Map). Learning progress tracked per-user-per-article with completion percentage, quiz scores, and time spent.
  • Content freshness: Configurable review cycles per article (30/60/90/180 days). Automated notifications to content owners when review is due. Stale content flagged in UI with amber indicators.

15.4.8 Organizational Data Modeling

Business OS modules fundamentally depend on organizational hierarchy: teams, seats (roles), people, and reporting relationships. This data model underlies the Responsibility Map and scopes data access throughout all modules.

  • Hierarchy storage: Postgres ltree extension for efficient hierarchical queries (ancestors, descendants, subtree operations). Alternative: closure table if ltree proves limiting for complex org structures. Decision: start with ltree, migrate to closure table only if needed.
  • Seat-person separation: Seats (roles/functions) are distinct from people. A seat can be vacant, held by one person, or shared. A person can hold multiple seats. This is core EOS methodology and must be strictly modeled.
  • Access control: RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) layered on top of Supabase RLS. Team-scoped data: users only see data for teams they belong to. Seat-scoped permissions: certain actions require specific seat assignments (e.g., only the meeting facilitator can advance sections).
  • Audit trail: Trigger-based audit log on all Business OS tables. Records who changed what, when, and the before/after values. Required for quarterly reviews and compliance.
  • Polymorphic relationships: ItemLink table for cross-module references (e.g., linking an issue to a quarterly goal, linking an action item to a meeting section). Uses source_type/source_id + target_type/target_id pattern.

15.4.9 Onboarding Wizard — Time-to-First-Meeting Optimization

The Business Operating System comprises 18 modules — but a new customer does not need all 18 to get value. The onboarding wizard is designed around a single goal: run the first Weekly Sync meeting within 45 minutes of account creation. Everything else is deferred to progressive disclosure over weeks 1–4. Without this wizard, the setup burden would be equivalent to filling out a 15-page form before seeing any value — a churn death sentence for a $275–$750/mo product.

15.4.9.1 Minimum Viable Setup (6 Configuration Steps)

Analysis of the Weekly Sync meeting’s data dependencies reveals exactly 6 things that must exist before the first meeting can run. Everything else can be empty, defaulted, or created later.

# Configuration Step Why Required Minimum Data Est. Time
1 Company Profile Tenant entity, timezone, industry Name, industry vertical, timezone, company size (headcount range) 30 sec
2 Core Values Required for Team Fit, Reviews, and culture tracking. Defines organizational identity. 3–5 core values (text strings). AI suggests industry-relevant defaults. 2 min
3 Leadership Team Minimum 1 team to scope meetings, goals, and KPIs. Weekly Sync is always team-scoped. 1 team name (default: “Leadership Team”) + invite members by email 2 min
4 Seats (Org Chart Stub) Goals and KPIs must be assigned to seat owners. Meetings need a facilitator seat. Create 2–3 leadership seats: Strategic Leader, Operations Leader, + 1 custom. Assign people to seats. 3 min
5 KPI Dashboard (3–5 measurables) Weekly Sync reviews KPI status. Dashboard with zero measurables is useless. 3–5 KPIs with name, owner, goal value, and goal direction. AI suggests based on industry. 3 min
6 Meeting Template Selection Defines the Weekly Sync agenda sections and time allocations. Select from 3 pre-built templates (Standard 90-min, Compact 60-min, Extended 120-min). Customize later. 30 sec

Key insight: Quarterly Goals are deliberately not in the minimum setup. A team can run their first Weekly Sync without any quarterly goals — the goals section of the meeting will simply show “No quarterly goals set yet. Create your first goals after this meeting.” This removes the single most time-consuming setup step (goal creation requires strategic thinking, not data entry) and lets teams experience the meeting flow before committing to the full methodology.

Total minimum setup time: ~11 minutes. With AI assistance, this drops to ~6–8 minutes because steps 2 and 5 are pre-populated.

15.4.9.2 Wizard Flow (Step-by-Step)

The wizard is a full-screen modal overlay that appears after the first admin user completes account creation (post-payment, post-email-verification). It uses a linear stepper UI with back/next navigation, progress indicator, and the AI Assistant Agent embedded in a collapsible side panel.

Step Screen Title UI Components AI Assistance Skip Allowed?
1 Welcome & Company Info Company name input (pre-filled from Stripe checkout / ACH bank verification), industry dropdown (20 options, “Other” with free text), company size radio (1–10, 11–50, 51–200, 201–500, 500+), timezone auto-detected from Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone with manual override dropdown. None needed — factual inputs only. No
2 Define Your Core Values Editable list of 3–5 text inputs, pre-populated with AI suggestions. Add/remove buttons. Character limit: 50 per value. Tooltip: “These should be the 3–5 non-negotiable behaviors that define your culture.” AI suggests 5 core values based on industry selection from Step 1. Construction example: “Safety First, Craftsmanship, Accountability, Client Obsession, Continuous Improvement.” User edits freely. Yes (defaults to AI suggestions)
3 Create Your Leadership Team Team name input (default: “Leadership Team”), multi-email invite field with bulk paste support (comma or newline separated). Invited users receive email with magic link. Admin can also skip invites and add members later. AI explains: “Your leadership team typically includes the owner, GM/COO, heads of sales, operations, and finance. Invite anyone who should attend the weekly leadership meeting.” Partially (team name required, invites can be skipped)
4 Set Up Key Roles Visual org chart builder with drag-and-drop. Pre-created with 2 top-level seats: Strategic Leader (owner/CEO) and Operations Leader (GM/COO). “+ Add Role” button. Each seat has: title input, person assignment dropdown (from invited team members), responsibilities textarea (optional). Maximum 10 seats in wizard; more can be added post-setup. AI suggests seat structure based on industry and company size. Construction GC with 11–50 employees gets: Strategic Leader, Operations Leader, VP of Sales, Project Management Lead, Finance Lead. AI pre-fills responsibilities for each suggested seat. Yes (defaults to 2 pre-created seats)
5 Track Your Key Numbers KPI builder with pre-populated suggestions. Each KPI card shows: title, owner (dropdown from seats), goal value input, goal direction toggle (above/below/between), unit selector (currency, percentage, number, days). “+ Add KPI” button. “I’ll set these up later” link (creates 3 placeholder KPIs). AI suggests 5–8 KPIs based on industry and company size. Construction example: Revenue ($, goal: above $X), Gross Margin (%, goal: above 35%), Backlog (months, goal: between 3–6), AR Aging >60 Days ($, goal: below $Y), Active Projects (count, goal: above Z), Safety Incidents (count, goal: below 0), Client Satisfaction (1–10, goal: above 8). User picks 3–5 from suggestions. Yes (creates 3 placeholder KPIs)
6 Choose Your Meeting Format 3 template cards with visual time breakdown:
Standard (90 min): Check-in (5) → KPI Review (10) → Goal Review (10) → Announcements (5) → Issues (60) → Wrap-up (5)
Compact (60 min): Check-in (5) → KPI Review (5) → Goal Review (5) → Issues (40) → Wrap-up (5)
Extended (120 min): Full template + deep dives
Selected template highlighted with blue border. “Customize sections” expandable panel for power users.		 AI recommends template based on team size. Teams of 3–5: Compact. Teams of 6–10: Standard. Teams of 10+: Extended. No (must select one)
7 You’re Ready! Success screen with animated checkmark. Summary of what was configured. Two CTA buttons:
Primary: “Schedule Your First Weekly Sync” (opens calendar picker)
Secondary: “Explore Your Dashboard” (goes to main app)
Below CTAs: “What to do next” checklist (see 15.4.9.4 Post-Setup Roadmap).		 AI sends a welcome message in the chat: “Your Business Operating System is ready. Here’s what I suggest you do before your first meeting...” N/A

15.4.9.3 AI-Assisted Setup — Industry Templates

The AI Assistant Agent uses the industry selection from Step 1 to pre-populate suggestions throughout the wizard. These are not generic placeholders — they are curated, industry-specific defaults maintained in a onboarding_templates JSONB configuration table.

Industry Suggested Core Values Suggested Seats Suggested KPIs
General Contractor Safety First, Craftsmanship, Accountability, Client Obsession, Continuous Improvement Strategic Leader, Operations Leader, VP of Sales, PM Lead, Finance Lead, Safety Director Revenue, Gross Margin, Backlog (months), AR >60 Days, Active Projects, Safety Incidents, Client Satisfaction Score
Subcontractor (Trade) Quality Work, Reliability, Safety, Teamwork, Integrity Owner, Operations Mgr, Estimating Lead, Field Supervisor, Office Manager Revenue, Win Rate (%), Avg Job Margin, Crew Utilization (%), Callbacks/Warranty Claims, TRIR
Design-Build Design Excellence, Client Partnership, Innovation, Integrity, Sustainable Practices Principal, Design Director, Project Director, Business Dev Lead, Finance Lead Revenue, Design-to-Build Conversion %, Avg Project Value, Client NPS, Repeat Client %, Design Revision Count
Remodeling / K&B Craftsmanship, Client Experience, Timeliness, Transparency, Pride in Work Owner, Lead Designer, Production Manager, Sales Lead, Office Manager Revenue, Lead-to-Close %, Avg Job Size, On-Time Completion %, Client Referral Rate, Change Order %
Real Estate / Property Client First, Market Expertise, Transparency, Responsiveness, Community Broker/Owner, Sales Manager, Marketing Lead, Operations Lead, Transaction Coordinator Closed Volume ($), Units Closed, Avg Days on Market, Listing Inventory, Lead Conversion %, Agent Productivity
Professional Services Client Success, Expertise, Integrity, Collaboration, Innovation Managing Partner, Client Services Lead, Operations Lead, Finance Lead, People Lead Revenue, Utilization Rate (%), Client Retention, NPS, Revenue per Employee, Avg Engagement Size
Other / Custom Integrity, Excellence, Teamwork, Innovation, Customer Focus CEO/Owner, COO/GM, Sales Lead, Operations Lead, Finance Lead Revenue, Gross Margin, Customer Retention, Employee Satisfaction, Cash Runway (months)

AI Pre-Population Mechanics

The AI Assistant Agent does not use live LLM calls for suggestion generation during onboarding — this would add latency and unpredictability to a critical first-impression flow. Instead:

  • Static templates: The onboarding_templates table stores curated suggestions per industry, loaded on wizard initialization. Zero latency, deterministic output.
  • AI refinement (optional): If the user clicks “Customize with AI” on any step, the AI Assistant Agent activates with a conversational interface: “Tell me about your business and I’ll suggest better KPIs.” This uses the standard AI gateway (Section 4) with Claude Haiku for fast responses (~500ms).
  • Learning from edits: When users modify AI suggestions, the deltas are logged to onboarding_analytics for future template refinement. If 80%+ of GCs change “Continuous Improvement” to “Never Settle,” the template updates.
TypeScript
// Onboarding template lookup — zero LLM latency
interface OnboardingTemplate {
  industry: IndustryVertical;
  companySize: CompanySizeRange;
  coreValues: string[];
  suggestedSeats: { title: string; seatType: SeatType; responsibilities: string[] }[];
  suggestedKPIs: { title: string; unit: KPIUnit; goalDirection: GoalDirection; goalValue: number | null }[];
  meetingRecommendation: 'compact' | 'standard' | 'extended';
}

async function getOnboardingTemplate(
  industry: IndustryVertical,
  companySize: CompanySizeRange
): Promise<OnboardingTemplate> {
  const template = await db
    .selectFrom('onboarding_templates')
    .where('industry', '=', industry)
    .where('company_size', '=', companySize)
    .executeTakeFirst();

  if (!template) {
    // Fallback to "Other / Custom" template
    return getOnboardingTemplate('other', companySize);
  }
  return template;
}

15.4.9.4 Import Capability

Ninety.io Import: Not Viable

Ninety.io offers no export API, no CSV export, no webhook access, and no data portability features. The only way to extract data is manual copy-paste from the Ninety.io web interface. This is a deliberate lock-in strategy by Ninety. Kiptra cannot build an automated import from Ninety.io — but this limitation is also a competitive advantage: customers who switch to Kiptra are making a commitment, reducing churn risk.

Available import paths (wizard Step 1 offers these options):

Import Source Method Data Imported Feasibility
Ninety.io Manual re-entry only N/A Not viable
Spreadsheet (CSV/XLSX) File upload with column mapping UI Team members (name, email, title), KPI definitions (name, owner, goal), quarterly goals (title, owner, status) Supported
Google Sheets OAuth2 + Sheets API v4, sheet selection picker Same as CSV, with live column preview Supported
Existing Kiptra Data Auto-pull from Kiptra CRM contacts and project data Team members from CRM contacts, KPI values from project financial data, company info from tenant record Supported

CSV/XLSX import implementation: The import flow uses a 3-step process: (1) file upload with drag-and-drop, (2) column mapping UI where users match their columns to Kiptra fields (AI auto-detects common column names like “Name,” “Email,” “Title”), and (3) preview/confirm with validation errors highlighted in red. Import runs as a BullMQ job to handle large files without blocking the UI. Maximum file size: 10 MB / 5,000 rows.

15.4.9.5 Post-Setup Roadmap (Progressive Disclosure)

After the wizard completes, users see a persistent “Getting Started” checklist in the sidebar that progressively introduces modules over 4 weeks. The checklist uses completion tracking stored in onboarding_progress (JSONB on the tenant_eos_settings table).

Timeframe Checklist Items Modules Unlocked AI Nudge
Day 1 (Post-Wizard)
  • Schedule your first Weekly Sync
  • Enter this week’s KPI values
  • Create 1–2 test issues in the Issue Tracker
 Meeting Hub, KPI Dashboard, Issue Tracker In-app notification 2 hours post-setup: “Ready to enter your first KPI values? Tap here to update your dashboard.”
Week 1
  • Run your first Weekly Sync meeting
  • Review the AI-generated meeting recap
  • Complete any carry-forward action items
  • Post your first Announcement (win or update)
 Weekly Action Items, Announcements Day before scheduled meeting: “Your first Weekly Sync is tomorrow. Here’s what to expect...” (AI sends a prep brief)
Week 2
  • Set 3–5 Quarterly Goals for this quarter
  • Add milestones to at least 1 goal
  • Add any missing team members
  • Review your KPI trends (2 data points now)
 Quarterly Goals, Goal Milestones After 2nd meeting: “You’ve run 2 Weekly Syncs — great rhythm! Now it’s time to set quarterly goals. Want me to help you write SMART goals?”
Week 3
  • Complete your Responsibility Map (all key seats filled)
  • Start your Strategic Vision Plan (10-year target + 3-year picture)
  • Connect your calendar (Google or Outlook)
 Strategic Vision Plan (full edit), Calendar Sync After 3rd meeting: “Your execution loop is running. Let’s build your strategic foundation — start with your 10-year target.”
Month 1
  • Review My Dashboard for personal view
  • Set up automated KPI data feeds (QuickBooks, CRM)
  • Explore the AI Assistant for goal suggestions
  • Schedule your first Quarterly Review (if near quarter-end)
 My Dashboard, AI Assistant, Analytics Dashboard preview End of month: “You’ve completed 4 Weekly Syncs. Your team’s meeting effectiveness score is X/10. Here’s what top-performing teams do differently...”

15.4.9.6 Onboarding Analytics & Drop-off Detection

Every wizard step tracks timing and completion status. If a user abandons the wizard mid-flow, the system detects it and triggers recovery actions.

Event Detection Automated Response
Wizard abandoned at Step 1–3 No activity for 24 hours after account creation Email: “You’re 3 minutes from your first dashboard. Pick up where you left off.” Deep link to wizard at last completed step.
Wizard abandoned at Step 4–6 No activity for 12 hours after last wizard interaction Email + in-app push: “Your team is almost ready. Finish setting up your KPIs and you can run your first meeting.”
Wizard completed, no meeting scheduled 48 hours post-wizard with no meeting created AI Assistant chat message: “I noticed you haven’t scheduled your first Weekly Sync yet. Most teams schedule theirs for Monday or Tuesday mornings. Want me to help?”
First meeting scheduled but not held Meeting scheduled time + 24 hours, no start event Email: “Your Weekly Sync was scheduled for yesterday. Things happen — want to reschedule for this week?”
First meeting held, no second meeting 7 days after first meeting, no recurring meeting or next meeting created Email: “Great first meeting! The power of the Weekly Sync comes from consistency. Set up a recurring weekly meeting to build the habit.” 
SQL
-- Onboarding tracking columns on tenant_eos_settings table
ALTER TABLE tenant_eos_settings ADD COLUMN onboarding_state JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{
  "wizard_started_at": null,
  "wizard_completed_at": null,
  "current_step": 1,
  "steps_completed": [],
  "step_durations_ms": {},
  "ai_suggestions_accepted": 0,
  "ai_suggestions_modified": 0,
  "ai_suggestions_rejected": 0,
  "import_source": null,
  "first_meeting_scheduled_at": null,
  "first_meeting_held_at": null,
  "checklist_progress": {}
}';

-- Index for drop-off detection queries
CREATE INDEX idx_tenant_eos_settings_onboarding_incomplete
  ON tenant_eos_settings ((onboarding_state->>'wizard_completed_at') IS NULL)
  WHERE (onboarding_state->>'wizard_completed_at') IS NULL;

15.4.9.7 Technical Implementation

Component Technology Details
Wizard UI Next.js App Router + Zustand Route: /app/onboarding. Full-screen layout (no sidebar). Zustand store for wizard state with localStorage persistence (survives browser refresh). 7 step components lazy-loaded via React.lazy().
Progress Persistence Supabase (tenant_eos_settings table) Each step completion writes to onboarding_state JSONB via Hono API. If user closes browser and returns, wizard resumes at last completed step.
Email Invite System SendGrid + better-auth magic links Step 3 invites trigger bos.onboarding.invite NATS event. Notification service sends branded invite emails with magic link that auto-joins the team on click.
AI Side Panel AI Assistant Agent (Claude Haiku via Vercel AI Gateway) Collapsible panel on right side of wizard. Conversational interface for “Customize with AI” requests. System prompt includes industry context and current wizard state. Uses streaming for responsive UX.
Import Pipeline BullMQ + Papa Parse (CSV) + SheetJS (XLSX) File uploaded to Supabase Storage, BullMQ job parses and validates, results stored in staging table for user confirmation. Max 10 MB / 5,000 rows.
Drop-off Detection BullMQ repeatable job Runs every 6 hours. Queries tenant_eos_settings where onboarding_state.wizard_completed_at IS NULL and created_at exceeds threshold. Publishes bos.onboarding.abandoned events to NATS for notification routing.
Post-Setup Checklist React component + tenant_eos_settings.onboarding_state Sidebar widget reads checklist_progress from onboarding_state. Each item maps to a route or action. Completed items show green checkmark. Widget auto-hides after all Month 1 items completed.

Decisions locked:

1. Wizard-first, not dashboard-first. New users see the wizard immediately after account creation, not the main dashboard. Rationale: an empty dashboard with zero data is worse than a guided setup flow.

2. Quarterly goals deferred. Goals are excluded from the minimum viable setup. Users create goals after their first meeting, when they understand the system. This reduces time-to-first-meeting by ~15–20 minutes.

3. Static templates over live AI. Onboarding suggestions come from curated templates, not live LLM calls. Rationale: predictability and zero-latency matter more than personalization during first-run UX. Live AI is available as an opt-in refinement.

4. 6-step minimum, not 18-module full setup. Only 6 of 18 modules require configuration before first use. The remaining 12 modules are introduced progressively over 4 weeks via the sidebar checklist.

15.4.10 Weekly Execution Loop — End-to-End Data Flow

The weekly execution loop is the core Business OS heartbeat: set quarterly goals → track KPIs weekly → meet weekly to review → resolve issues → assign action items → repeat. This section specifies the complete data flow for one cycle, from Monday KPI entry through the Weekly Sync meeting through action item completion.

Flow
SEQUENCE DIAGRAM: One Complete Weekly Cycle
===========================================

Mon 9 AM             Mon–Thu              Thu 3 PM (pre-meeting)     Thu 4 PM (Weekly Sync)                          Fri
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  v                    v                      v                          v                                               v

[KPI Owner]     [KPI/Scorecard Agent]   [Meeting Facilitator]      [Meeting Facilitator]                          [Goal & Alignment]
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |  POST /api/bos/    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |  measurables/:id/  |                      |                          |                                               |
  |  values            |                      |                          |                                               |
  |--->[Supabase]      |                      |                          |                                               |
  |    INSERT INTO      |                      |                          |                                               |
  |    measurable_values|                      |                          |                                               |
  |         |          |                      |                          |                                               |
  |         |--NOTIFY-->|                      |                          |                                               |
  |         |  pg_notify|                      |                          |                                               |
  |         |  'kpi_    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |         |  updated' |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |              [analyzeTrend]               |                          |                                               |
  |              Compare value vs             |                          |                                               |
  |              goal_value, check            |                          |                                               |
  |              goal_direction               |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |              IF off-track:                |                          |                                               |
  |              NATS publish                 |                          |                                               |
  |              TENANT_{id}.bos.             |                          |                                               |
  |              kpi.off_track                |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |----NATS event------->|                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                [auto-add to next                |                                               |
  |                    |                 meeting's issues                |                                               |
  |                    |                 section via                     |                                               |
  |                    |                 INSERT INTO issues              |                                               |
  |                    |                 + item_links]                   |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  BullMQ cron job         |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (T-60min before         |                                               |
  |                    |                      |   meeting):              |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  [prepareMeetingAgenda]  |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  READ: measurables       |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (off-track this week)   |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  READ: quarterly_goals   |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (status != complete)    |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  READ: action_items      |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (due <= this week)      |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  READ: issues            |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (status = open)         |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  READ: headlines         |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  (created this week)     |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  NATS publish:           |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  TENANT_{id}.bos.        |                                               |
  |                    |                      |  meeting.agenda_ready    |                                               |
  |                    |                      |         |                |                                               |
  |                    |                      |         |---Socket.io-->|  [Attendees see agenda]                       |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                    [startMeeting]                                         |
  |                    |                      |                    XState: scheduled → in_progress                      |
  |                    |                      |                    NATS: TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.started                  |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 1: Segue/Check-in (5 min)                      |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 2: KPI Review (5 min)                    |
  |                    |                      |                      READ: mv_kpi_weekly_summary                         |
  |                    |                      |                      (materialized view, refreshed 15min)                |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 3: Goal Review (5 min)                         |
  |                    |                      |                      READ: quarterly_goals WHERE status IN               |
  |                    |                      |                      ('on_track','off_track','at_risk')                  |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 4: Action Item Review (5 min)                  |
  |                    |                      |                      READ: action_items WHERE due_date                   |
  |                    |                      |                      <= current_week AND completed_at IS NULL            |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 5: Headlines (5 min)                           |
  |                    |                      |                      READ: headlines WHERE created_at >= week_start      |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 6: Issue Resolution — Identify, Discuss, Resolve (60 min)  |
  |                    |                      |                      READ: issues ORDER BY priority, vote_count           |
  |                    |                      |                      For each resolved issue:                             |
  |                    |                      |                        UPDATE issues SET status='resolved'                |
  |                    |                      |                        INSERT INTO action_items (from resolution)         |
  |                    |                      |                    Section 7: Conclude (5 min)                            |
  |                    |                      |                      [collectRating] per attendee (1–10)                  |
  |                    |                      |                      [advanceSection] → XState: wrapping_up             |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                    [generateRecap] (Haiku LLM call)                       |
  |                    |                      |                      INPUT: meeting_agenda_sections.notes,               |
  |                    |                      |                             resolved issues, new action_items            |
  |                    |                      |                      OUTPUT: recap_html on meetings row                  |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                    [extractActionItems] (structured output)               |
  |                    |                      |                      INSERT INTO action_items                            |
  |                    |                      |                      (source_meeting_id = this meeting)                  |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                    NATS publish:                                         |
  |                    |                      |                    TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.completed                     |
  |                    |                      |                    payload: {meeting_id, action_item_ids[],              |
  |                    |                      |                             resolved_issue_ids[], rating_avg}            |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |--NATS--> [Goal & Alignment Agent]              |
  |                    |                      |                          |          Consumes meeting.completed            |
  |                    |                      |                          |          [updateProgress] for goals            |
  |                    |                      |                          |          linked to resolved issues             |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |--NATS--> [Notification Service]                |
  |                    |                      |                          |          Email recap to all attendees          |
  |                    |                      |                          |          Socket.io: action_item assignments    |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                          [Fri]|
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    Action item |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    owners work |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    on tasks.   |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    PATCH /api/ |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    bos/actions |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    /:id        |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    {completed_ |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                    at: now()}   |
  |                    |                      |                          |                                               |
NEXT MONDAY: Cycle repeats. Incomplete action_items carry forward (carried_from_id = original id).
Weekly Sync Duration Deviation Note

The Kiptra Weekly Sync template extends KPI Review and Goal Review to 10 minutes each (vs standard 5 minutes in the canonical EOS Level 10 Meeting™ agenda) to accommodate richer data visualization and AI-generated trend commentary. Total meeting time remains 90 minutes; the additional 10 minutes are reclaimed from the Issue Resolution section (50 min vs standard 60 min), reflecting that automated KPI flagging resolves some issues before the meeting begins.

NATS Event Table

SubjectPublisherConsumer(s)Payload SummaryTrigger
TENANT_{id}.bos.kpi.value_recorded Hono API (POST handler) KPI/Scorecard Agent { measurable_id, value, week_start, entered_by } User enters weekly KPI value
TENANT_{id}.bos.kpi.off_track KPI/Scorecard Agent Meeting Facilitator Agent, Notification Service { measurable_id, title, value, goal_value, goal_direction, weeks_off_track, owner_id, team_id } KPI value misses goal threshold
TENANT_{id}.bos.kpi.trend_alert KPI/Scorecard Agent AI Assistant Agent, Notification Service { measurable_id, trend_direction, consecutive_weeks, severity } 3+ consecutive weeks declining
TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.agenda_ready Meeting Facilitator Agent Notification Service { meeting_id, team_id, scheduled_at, off_track_count, open_issues_count, overdue_actions_count } BullMQ cron T-60min before meeting
TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.started Meeting Facilitator Agent Notification Service (Socket.io broadcast) { meeting_id, facilitator_id, attendee_ids[], template_id } Facilitator clicks Start Meeting
TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.section_advanced Meeting Facilitator Agent Socket.io (live UI update) { meeting_id, from_section, to_section, actual_duration_seconds } Section timer expires or manual advance
TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.issue_resolved Meeting Facilitator Agent Goal & Alignment Agent, Notification Service { meeting_id, issue_id, resolution_notes, action_item_ids[] } Issue marked resolved during Issue Resolution Process
TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.completed Meeting Facilitator Agent Goal & Alignment Agent, KPI/Scorecard Agent, Notification Service, AI Assistant Agent { meeting_id, action_item_ids[], resolved_issue_ids[], rating_avg, recap_html, duration_minutes } Meeting concludes and recap generated
TENANT_{id}.bos.action.created Hono API / Meeting Facilitator Notification Service, Goal & Alignment Agent { action_item_id, owner_id, title, due_date, source_meeting_id, linked_goal_id } Action item extracted from meeting or created manually
TENANT_{id}.bos.action.completed Hono API (PATCH handler) Goal & Alignment Agent, KPI/Scorecard Agent { action_item_id, owner_id, completed_at } Owner marks action item done
TENANT_{id}.bos.action.overdue BullMQ cron (daily 8 AM tenant-local) Notification Service, Meeting Facilitator Agent { action_item_ids[], owner_ids[], days_overdue[] } Daily overdue check job
TENANT_{id}.bos.goal.progress_updated Goal & Alignment Agent KPI/Scorecard Agent (cross-reference), Notification Service { goal_id, previous_progress_pct, new_progress_pct, status, updated_by } Milestone completed or manual progress update
TENANT_{id}.bos.goal.status_changed Goal & Alignment Agent Meeting Facilitator Agent (auto-add to agenda), Notification Service { goal_id, previous_status, new_status, owner_id } Goal transitions to off_track or at_risk

Database Operations by Phase

PhaseTables ReadTables WrittenTriggers / Side Effects
Monday: KPI Entry measurables (goal_value, goal_direction) measurable_values (INSERT), audit_logs (trigger) pg_notify kpi_updated channel → KPI/Scorecard Agent subscriber. Valkey cache invalidation for tenant:{id}:kpi:dashboard.
Mon–Thu: Off-Track Detection measurable_values (last 13 weeks), measurables (thresholds) issues (INSERT if auto-escalation enabled), item_links (link issue → measurable), notifications (INSERT) NATS publish bos.kpi.off_track. Materialized view mv_kpi_weekly_summary refreshed by BullMQ cron every 15 minutes.
Pre-Meeting Prep (T-60min) meetings, meeting_templates, measurables + measurable_values, quarterly_goals + milestones, action_items, issues, headlines meeting_agenda_sections (INSERT/UPDATE with pre-populated data snapshots) BullMQ scheduled job per team. Reads from materialized view mv_kpi_weekly_summary instead of raw tables. NATS publish bos.meeting.agenda_ready.
During Meeting: Section Progression meeting_agenda_sections (current section), meetings.workflow_state (XState) meetings.workflow_state (UPDATE per transition), meeting_agenda_sections.started_at, meeting_agenda_sections.ended_at XState state machine persisted to workflow_state JSONB. BullMQ delayed job for section timer auto-advance. Socket.io broadcast on every section change.
During Meeting: Issue Resolution (IRP) issues (prioritized list), item_links (linked goals/actions) issues (UPDATE status='resolved', resolution_notes), action_items (INSERT from resolution), item_links (INSERT issue → action_item), audit_logs NATS publish bos.meeting.issue_resolved per resolved issue. Goal & Alignment Agent consumes to check if resolution unblocks a quarterly goal.
Meeting Conclusion: Recap meeting_agenda_sections (all notes), meeting_attendees (ratings), issues (resolved this meeting), action_items (created this meeting) meetings.recap_html (UPDATE), meetings.average_rating (UPDATE), meetings.ended_at (UPDATE), meeting_attendees.rating (UPDATE per attendee) LLM call (Haiku) generates recap from section notes. extractActionItems uses structured output to parse unresolved discussion into action items. NATS publish bos.meeting.completed. Notification service emails recap to all attendees.
Post-Meeting: Goal Updates action_items (new from meeting), item_links (action → goal), quarterly_goals, milestones quarterly_goals.progress_pct (UPDATE via rollup), quarterly_goals.status (UPDATE if progress triggers threshold) Goal & Alignment Agent consumes meeting.completed event. Recalculates progress_pct from milestone completion + action item completion. NATS publish bos.goal.progress_updated.
Fri–Mon: Action Item Execution action_items (assigned to user) action_items.completed_at (UPDATE), audit_logs NATS publish bos.action.completed. Daily BullMQ cron checks for overdue items, publishes bos.action.overdue. Incomplete items at next meeting carry forward: new row with carried_from_id referencing original.
Materialized View Refresh Source tables: measurable_values, measurables, quarterly_goals, action_items mv_kpi_weekly_summary, mv_goal_completion_rates, mv_meeting_effectiveness BullMQ cron: every 15 minutes for active data, hourly for historical. REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY (non-blocking). Valkey cache bust on refresh via pg_notify. Live meeting override: When a Weekly Sync meeting is in-progress, KPI value changes trigger an immediate NATS event TENANT_{id}.bos.kpi.value_changed which forces an on-demand materialized view refresh within 30 seconds, bypassing the 15-minute cron. Prevents stale data during live meeting KPI review segments.

Agent Handoff Chains

Four concrete handoff patterns operate during the weekly cycle:

Flow
HANDOFF 1: KPI Off-Track → Meeting Agenda
=============================================
KPI/Scorecard Agent
  → analyzeTrend(measurable_id)
  → Detects: value 72, goal ≥ 80, direction = 'above'
  → Publishes: TENANT_{id}.bos.kpi.off_track
      payload: { measurable_id, title: "Close Rate", value: 72,
                 goal_value: 80, weeks_off_track: 2, owner_id, team_id }

Meeting Facilitator Agent (subscriber)
  → Consumes: bos.kpi.off_track
  → INSERT INTO issues (title: "Close Rate off-track (72% vs 80% goal, 2 weeks)",
       team_id, category: 'kpi_auto', priority: 3)
  → INSERT INTO item_links (source_type: 'issue', target_type: 'measurable',
       link_type: 'caused_by')
  → Issue appears in next Weekly Sync Issue Resolution section automatically


HANDOFF 2: Meeting Resolution → Goal Progress
================================================
Meeting Facilitator Agent
  → extractActionItems(meeting_agenda_sections.notes)
  → Structured output (Haiku): extracts owner, title, due_date per action
  → INSERT INTO action_items (source_meeting_id = meeting.id)
  → For issues linked to quarterly_goals:
       INSERT INTO item_links (source_type: 'action_item', target_type: 'quarterly_goal')
  → Publishes: TENANT_{id}.bos.meeting.completed

Goal & Alignment Agent (subscriber)
  → Consumes: bos.meeting.completed
  → For each resolved_issue_id in payload:
       READ item_links WHERE source_id = issue_id AND target_type = 'quarterly_goal'
       If linked goal found:
         Recalculate progress_pct from milestones + linked action_items
         UPDATE quarterly_goals SET progress_pct = new_value
  → Publishes: TENANT_{id}.bos.goal.progress_updated


HANDOFF 3: Goal Status Change → Meeting Agenda
=================================================
Goal & Alignment Agent
  → progress_pct drops below 25% at week 8 of quarter
  → UPDATE quarterly_goals SET status = 'at_risk'
  → Publishes: TENANT_{id}.bos.goal.status_changed
      payload: { goal_id, previous_status: 'on_track', new_status: 'at_risk' }

Meeting Facilitator Agent (subscriber)
  → Consumes: bos.goal.status_changed WHERE new_status IN ('at_risk', 'off_track')
  → INSERT INTO issues (title: "Q2 Goal at risk: [goal title] (25% at week 8)",
       category: 'goal_auto', priority: 2)


HANDOFF 4: AI Assistant Cross-Domain Query
===========================================
User: "How did we do this quarter?"

AI Assistant Agent
  → Tool calls (read-only):
       1. queryGoals({ quarter: current, status: all }) → quarterly_goals
       2. queryKPIs({ period: 'trailing_13_week' }) → mv_kpi_weekly_summary
       3. queryMeetings({ quarter: current }) → meetings (avg rating, issue count)
       4. queryActions({ quarter: current }) → action_items (completion rate)
  → Synthesizes via Sonnet:
       "Q2 summary: 7/10 Quarterly Goals on track (70%), avg meeting rating 8.2,
        142/168 action items completed (84.5%), 3 KPIs consistently off-track:
        Close Rate, Lead Response Time, Gross Margin."

Error and Retry Paths

Failure ScenarioDetectionRecovery PathUser Impact
KPI data source unavailable (external API — QuickBooks, CRM) KPI/Scorecard Agent ingestExternalData tool returns connection error or timeout (>30s) BullMQ retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 5min/15min/60min). After 3 failures: mark measurable as source: 'stale', NATS publish bos.kpi.data_source_failed, create notification for KPI owner: "[Source] data unavailable — enter value manually or retry." Manual entry fallback always available. KPI shows last known value with amber "stale" indicator. Owner can enter manually. Meeting proceeds with stale data flagged.
Meeting abandoned mid-session (facilitator disconnects, browser crash) XState machine in in_progress state with no section_advanced event for >15 minutes. Socket.io disconnect event for facilitator. BullMQ health check queries meetings WHERE workflow_state->>'value' = 'in_progress' AND updated_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes'. NATS publish bos.meeting.stalled. Notification to all attendees: "Meeting appears stalled — resume or end?" If no response in 10 minutes: XState transition to paused. Meeting state fully preserved in workflow_state JSONB — facilitator can resume from exact section. No data loss: all section notes, resolved issues, and action items created before the stall are persisted. Attendees see "Meeting paused" state. Can resume or end with partial recap. All in-progress work preserved.
Action item overdue (>7 days past due) Daily BullMQ cron job (8 AM tenant-local) queries action_items WHERE completed_at IS NULL AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE. Day 1 overdue: in-app notification + email to owner. Day 3 overdue: escalation notification to owner's manager (via seats → parent_seat_id hierarchy). Day 7+ overdue: auto-create issue in next meeting's Issue Resolution section (title: "Overdue action item: [title] (7+ days)", category: 'accountability'). At next meeting: incomplete items carry forward with carried_from_id reference. Carry count tracked for accountability reporting. Owner receives escalating notifications. Item persists across meetings until resolved. Carry count visible in meeting recap and analytics.
LLM failure during recap generation generateRecap Haiku call returns error or timeout (>60s) Retry once with same context. If second failure: store meeting data without recap (recap_html = NULL), mark meeting as completed_no_recap. Queue BullMQ job to retry recap generation in 30 minutes. NATS publish bos.meeting.recap_failed. Notification: "Meeting completed. AI recap will be available shortly." Meeting concludes normally. Recap arrives late (email delayed). All action items and resolutions captured regardless of recap status.
Materialized view refresh failure BullMQ job for REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY fails (lock contention, OOM) Retry in 5 minutes. Dashboard falls back to Valkey-cached stale data (TTL extended from 5min to 30min on failure). Alert to operations channel. Views remain queryable with stale data — CONCURRENTLY ensures the old snapshot is served during refresh. Dashboard shows slightly stale data (up to 30 min old). No user-visible error.

Key insight: The weekly loop is resilient by design — every failure mode has a manual fallback that preserves the meeting cadence. KPI data can be entered manually, meetings can be paused and resumed, action items carry forward across weeks, and recaps can be regenerated asynchronously. The methodology works even when the AI layer is degraded.

15.5 Database Schema

The Business Operating System adds approximately 30+ new tables to the Kiptra database, organized by domain. All tables follow Kiptra conventions: UUID primary keys, tenant_id for multi-tenancy, created_at/updated_at timestamps, and soft-delete via deleted_at.

Key insight: All Business OS tables inherit Kiptra’s tenant isolation model: Row-Level Security (RLS) policies on every table, tenant_id column indexed and enforced, and all queries wrapped in withTenantContext() (see Section 3). No additional multi-tenancy work is required — the pattern is already established.

Architecture Decision: Rename companies to tenant_eos_settings (ADR-SCHEMA-001)

Decision: Option C — Rename companies to tenant_eos_settings — a clearly scoped settings table with a 1:1 foreign key to tenants.

CriterionA: Merge into tenantsB: companies FK→tenantsC: tenant_eos_settings (chosen)
Schema clarity Muddies the core table — tenants becomes a dumping ground for every module’s config. Ambiguous naming — companies sounds like a parallel top-level entity. Best. Name explicitly communicates: “this is EOS-specific configuration for a tenant.”
Migration safety Requires ALTER TABLE on the high-traffic tenants table. Clean — new table, no core table changes. Best. New table, zero changes to tenants.
Future extensibility Every new module adds more columns to tenants — “God table” anti-pattern. Sets a precedent but naming doesn’t follow a pattern. Best. Establishes the tenant_{module}_settings naming convention. K&B can add tenant_kb_settings.

Key constraint: The tenant_eos_settings table has a UNIQUE constraint on tenant_id, enforcing 1:1 cardinality. It is NOT a child table — it is a settings extension. RLS policy uses the same tenant_id pattern as all other tables.

Updated CREATE TABLE (replaces companies)

SQL
-- EOS-specific tenant configuration (1:1 extension of tenants table)
-- Replaces the 'companies' table from Section 15.5
CREATE TABLE tenant_eos_settings (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  tenant_id         UUID REFERENCES tenants NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  name              TEXT,                          -- Display name for EOS context (may differ from tenants.name)
  core_values       JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',   -- Array of {value, description} objects
  mission           TEXT,
  niche             TEXT,
  target_market     TEXT,
  fiscal_year_start INTEGER DEFAULT 1,             -- Month (1-12), default January
  quarter_start_month INTEGER DEFAULT 1,           -- Allows offset quarters
  settings          JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',   -- Module-level settings (meeting defaults, scorecard config, etc.)
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- RLS: same tenant isolation pattern as all other tables
ALTER TABLE tenant_eos_settings ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON tenant_eos_settings
  USING (tenant_id = (SELECT current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid));

Cascading Updates Required

  • All Section 15.5 tables referencing company_id must be updated to reference tenant_id directly (not through the settings table). The teams, vision_plans, long_term_issues, org_charts tables already have tenant_id for RLS — the company_id FK is redundant and should be removed.
  • No ALTER TABLE on tenants required. The tenants table already has all needed columns.
  • teams table update: Change company_id to tenant_id (column already exists for RLS, drop the company_id FK).

Organizational Tables (5)

Table Purpose Key Columns
tenant_eos_settings Business OS module configuration (1:1 tenant extension) tenant_id (UNIQUE), name, core_values (JSONB), mission, niche, target_market, fiscal_year_start, settings (JSONB)
teams Departments/functional groups tenant_id, name, parent_team_id, path (ltree)
team_memberships User-to-team assignments user_id, team_id, role (enum: member/admin/owner)
user_profiles Extended user data for Business OS user_id, display_name, avatar_url, title, bio, core_values_scores (JSONB)
user_workspace_layouts Per-user dashboard layouts user_id, workspace (enum), layout (JSONB — React Grid Layout config)

Strategic Tables (5)

Table Purpose Key Columns
vision_plans Strategic Vision Plan (V/TO equivalent) tenant_id, version, ten_year_target, three_year_picture (JSONB), one_year_plan (JSONB), status
long_term_issues Issues parked for future quarters tenant_id, title, description, target_quarter, priority
org_charts Responsibility Map versions tenant_id, version, is_active, layout_data (JSONB)
seats Roles/functions in the org chart org_chart_id, title, parent_seat_id, path (ltree), responsibilities (JSONB), seat_type (enum)
seat_assignments Person-to-seat mapping seat_id, user_id, is_primary, assigned_at, removed_at

Execution Tables (5)

Table Purpose Key Columns
quarterly_goals 90-day priorities (EOS methodology equivalent) tenant_id, team_id, owner_id, title, description, quarter, year, status, parent_goal_id, progress_pct
milestones Sub-goals within quarterly goals goal_id, title, due_date, status, sort_order
action_items Weekly to-do items owner_id, team_id, title, due_date, completed_at, source_meeting_id, carried_from_id
issues Issue Tracker entries team_id, title, description, category (enum), priority, status, resolved_at, resolution_notes
headlines Announcements and wins tenant_id, team_id, author_id, title, body, headline_type (enum: win/news/update)

Meeting Tables (4)

Table Purpose Key Columns
meeting_templates Reusable meeting structures tenant_id, name, meeting_type (enum), sections (JSONB — ordered section definitions with default durations)
meetings Individual meeting instances template_id, team_id, facilitator_id, scheduled_at, started_at, ended_at, workflow_state (JSONB), recap_html, average_rating
meeting_attendees Meeting participation meeting_id, user_id, status (enum: invited/accepted/declined/attended), rating (1–10)
meeting_agenda_sections Per-meeting section overrides and notes meeting_id, section_key, duration_minutes, notes (JSONB — ProseMirror doc), started_at, ended_at

KPI Tables (3)

Table Purpose Key Columns
scorecards KPI Dashboard definitions team_id, name, description, is_active
measurables Individual KPI definitions scorecard_id, owner_id, title, unit, goal_value, goal_direction (enum: above/below/between), goal_min, goal_max, data_source (enum: manual/api/computed), formula (JSONB)
measurable_values Weekly KPI data points measurable_id, week_start (date), value (numeric), entered_by, source (enum: manual/automated), notes

People & Review Tables (4)

Table Purpose Key Columns
reviews Quarterly review instances reviewee_id, reviewer_id, quarter, year, status (enum: draft/self_assessment/manager_review/discussion/finalized), workflow_state (JSONB)
review_responses Individual review answers review_id, question_id, response_type (enum: self/manager), score, comments
review_questions Configurable review questions tenant_id, category (enum: core_value/ccc/goal_retro/open_ended), question_text, sort_order
fit_checks Team Fit Assessment results user_id, assessor_id, quarter, year, core_values_scores (JSONB), competency (enum: +/+−/−), commitment (enum), capacity (enum)

Knowledge Portal Tables (10+)

Table Purpose Key Columns
knowledge_categories Top-level content organization tenant_id, name, description, icon, sort_order
knowledge_collections Groups of related articles category_id, name, description, prerequisite_collection_id
knowledge_articles Individual content pieces collection_id, title, slug, status (enum), estimated_minutes, review_cycle_days, last_reviewed_at, search_vector (tsvector)
content_blocks Article content blocks article_id, block_type (enum), content (JSONB), sort_order
content_versions Article version history article_id, version, blocks_snapshot (JSONB), changed_by, change_summary
content_assignments Seat-based article assignments article_id, seat_id, assigned_by, due_date, required (boolean)
user_progress Per-user learning progress user_id, article_id, progress_pct, completed_at, time_spent_seconds, quiz_score
sop_documents Standard operating procedures tenant_id, team_id, title, version, status (enum: draft/review/approved), approved_by, steps (JSONB)
assessments Business Health Assessment surveys tenant_id, assessment_type, quarter, year, status, results (JSONB)
assessment_responses Individual survey responses assessment_id, user_id, answers (JSONB), is_anonymous (boolean)

Infrastructure Tables (5+)

Table Purpose Key Columns
notifications In-app notification records user_id, type (enum), title, body, link, read_at, channel (enum), dedup_hash
notification_preferences Per-user, per-type channel toggles user_id, notification_type, in_app (boolean), email (boolean), push (boolean), digest_frequency (enum)
calendar_sync_connections External calendar OAuth connections user_id, provider (enum: google/microsoft), access_token_enc, refresh_token_enc, calendar_id, sync_token, last_synced_at
audit_logs Change history for all Business OS tables table_name, record_id, action (enum: insert/update/delete), old_values (JSONB), new_values (JSONB), changed_by, changed_at
item_links Polymorphic cross-module references source_type, source_id, target_type, target_id, link_type (enum: related/blocks/caused_by/resolves)

15.6 New Agents

The Business Operating System introduces seven new agents to the Kiptra agent ecosystem. Each agent is implemented as an AgentPluginFactory (Section 6) using the factory + registration pattern, and communicates via NATS JetStream event subjects defined in Section 5. All agents emit the standard 13-event streaming protocol (AI-25, Section 7b).

Agent Purpose Key Tools
Business OS Agent Core domain logic orchestrator for the entire Business OS module. Routes requests to specialized sub-agents, manages cross-module operations, and enforces business rules. createQuarterlyGoal, updateVisionPlan, resolveIssue, linkItems, getCompanyDashboard
Meeting Facilitator Agent Manages live meeting state machines. Controls section transitions, enforces time limits, collects ratings, and generates AI-powered meeting recaps with action item extraction. startMeeting, advanceSection, pauseMeeting, collectRating, generateRecap, extractActionItems
Goal & Alignment Agent Manages the quarterly goal lifecycle. Handles cascading alignment (company → team → individual), progress rollup calculations, milestone tracking, and quarter-end evaluation workflows. createGoal, cascadeGoal, updateProgress, evaluateQuarter, suggestGoals, checkAlignment
KPI/Scorecard Agent Manages KPI dashboards and measurables. Builds computed formulas, ingests automated data from external sources (QuickBooks, CRM), detects trends, and generates off-track alerts. createMeasurable, recordValue, buildFormula, ingestExternalData, analyzeTrend, alertOffTrack
People & Review Agent Manages quarterly review workflows and team fit assessments. Orchestrates the self-assessment → manager review → discussion → finalization pipeline. Tracks core values alignment over time. initiateReview, submitSelfAssessment, submitManagerReview, scheduleDiscussion, recordFitCheck, trackValuesTrend
Knowledge Portal Agent Manages content lifecycle in the Knowledge Portal. Handles article creation, version control, seat-based assignment, progress tracking, and content freshness enforcement cycles. createArticle, assignContent, trackProgress, checkFreshness, searchKnowledge, generateQuiz
AI Assistant Agent Conversational AI for Business OS tasks. Helps users create SMART quarterly goals, build seat descriptions, prepare meeting agendas, analyze KPI trends, and get recommendations on issue prioritization. createSMARTGoal, buildSeatDescription, prepareMeetingAgenda, analyzeKPIs, prioritizeIssues, summarizeQuarter

Key insight: All seven agents are implemented as AgentPluginFactory functions (Section 6) and communicate via NATS JetStream (Section 2). No changes to the core event bus, executor, agent dashboard, or agent lifecycle infrastructure are required. The Business OS agents are simply new factory functions registered in the agent registry, emitting the same 13-event streaming protocol (AI-25) as the operational agents.

Agent Interaction Patterns

Business OS agents frequently collaborate on cross-cutting operations:

  • Meeting → Execution: Meeting Facilitator extracts action items and issues during recap generation, then publishes events that the Goal & Alignment Agent and KPI/Scorecard Agent consume to update relevant records.
  • KPI → Meeting: KPI/Scorecard Agent identifies off-track measurables and automatically adds them to the next Weekly Sync’s issues section via the Meeting Facilitator Agent.
  • Review → People: People & Review Agent pulls goal completion data from Goal & Alignment Agent and KPI performance from KPI/Scorecard Agent to pre-populate review forms.
  • AI Assistant → All: AI Assistant Agent has read access to all other agents’ data via tool calls, enabling conversational queries like “How did our team do this quarter?” that span multiple domains.

15.7 Implementation Phases

The Business Operating System is built in three phases, each delivering a complete, usable increment of functionality.
